metadata.name: CyberArk Known Issues
metadata.description: CyberArk Known Issues database scraped from the CyberArk community portal

known_issues.title: KI#00024893: EPM Win Agent - Date & Time pane prompts UAC when open in windows 11
known_issues.description: Configuring settings within elevated Date & Time ICP Windows, triggers a Windows UAC Dialog message.
known_issues.workaround: Create Elevate On Demand policy for process: C:\WINDOWS\system32\SystemSettingsAdminFlows.exe with parameter - 'ForceTimeSync' Publisher is Microsoft Windows

known_issues.title: KI#00021685: PSM Recorder ignores codec settings and always encodes recordings as SCPR
known_issues.description: General description: If the codec is configured as MSVC, the recorder ignores this setting and encodes the videos using ScreenPressor, yet sets the FourCC value to MSVC, rendering the recordings unplayable in PVWA. Steps to reproduce: * In Configuration -> Privileged Session Management -> General Settings -> Recorder Settings, set the value of Codec to MSVC * Restart PSM to ensure the change has been applied * Use PSM to open and record an RDP session * Attempt to play the recording in PVWA Current Results: The recording does not play. Expected Results: Recording is encoded as MSVC and plays correctly.
known_issues.workaround: Option 1: only use SCPR as the codec setting. Option 2: play the files using Windows Media Player, after installing ScreenPressor codec. Option 3: after the recordings have been created, manually or using scripts open the files in binary mode, and replace the four bytes at position 68 to 71 to fix the corrupted FourCC value.

known_issues.title: KI#00025665: CyberArk Identity - User Portal API Calls failing with Error 400: Bad Request
known_issues.description: When Users perform actions within the User Portal, such as selecting 'Manage My Account' or following authentication, an error 'The Request Failed with Unhandled Error' may be presented and the page does not load. This occurs inconsistently. The cause relates to cached elements within the Browser, which causes UI resources to load before the Shell (ISPSS) initialization process has completed, resulting in a missing validation token causing Error 400: Bad Request.
known_issues.workaround: Use an incognito browser window (which has no cache). Users may also find that clearing the browser cache is an effective workaround.

known_issues.title: KI#00025680: EPM Console - SAML Provision. NG Roles - specific case for ALL sets is not handled
known_issues.description: SAML Provisioning. Old JSON can contain "ALL" value as SetID. This case was not handled in the new algorithm (NG Roles).
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00022198: update doc PAKeyGen 12.6 generate keys with VC++ Runtimes 2015-2022
KI#00014226: New RDP Proxy sessions to the PSM fail for a period of a few minutes
known_issues.description: All new RDP Proxy / PSM for Windows sessions fail. This occurs for a time period of about 5 minutes after which new RDP Proxy sessions can connect again. The issue repeats occasionally. Only RDP Proxy sessions are affected. The issue occurs when a session terminates unexpectedly at the logon phase. This prevents the OS Session ID from being reused by PSM until the next PSM periodic cleanup activity. New RDP Proxy sessions try using this Session ID which causes these sessions to fail.
known_issues.workaround: Wait for 5 minutes before trying to connect again.

known_issues.title: KI#00021503: During a Vault upgrade (12 and up) the installer mistakenly recodnize the MySQL as V5.6 instead of V8
known_issues.description: The Vault installer uses a file called VaultDBVersion.ver (located in \PrivateArk\Safes\Metadata) which simply stores the current MySQL version (5.6 or 8.0) so the upgrade procedure can tell which version is already installed. Once running the installer to upgrade a Vault 12 and up, the script mistakenly recognize the database as 5.6 and as a result tries to upgrade it to 5.7 (on its way to V8) although the database is already V8. If the file VaultDBVersion.ver is missing the default is 5.6.
known_issues.workaround: Edit VaultDBVersion.ver (located in \PrivateArk\Safes\Metadata) and write 8.0 in it

known_issues.title: KI#00024669: Kubernetes API host precedences prevents authn-k8s from working correctly across Kubernetes clusters
known_issues.description: The Conjur Kubernetes authenticator verifies the existence and properties of authenticating Kubernetes principals using the Kubernetes API. There are two ways the API URL is configured: 1) By the KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST environment variable, 2) By the kubernetes/api-url Conjur variable in the authenticator policy. Currently, the environment variable has first precedence. This prevents configuring a Conjur Follower in one cluster from authenticating a workload in another cluster. Generally, this would be accomplished by using the kubernetes/api-url to point the Follower to the correct cluster for resource validation. However, because the Follower is running as a Kubernetes Pod, Kubernetes provides its own KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST to the Follower container, overriding the policy configuration. The policy configuration, if provided, should take highest precedence instead.
known_issues.workaround: Two viable workarounds: 1) Use JWT authentication rather than certificate authentication (JWT auth does not rely on these environment variables). 2) If using the Conjur Appliance Follower in Kubernetes, clear the environment configuration inside the Follower container: echo "KUBERNETES_SERVICE_HOST=" >> /opt/conjur/etc/conjur.conf && sv restart conjur. This forces Conjur to use the policy configuration. Note: There is no equivalent workaround for the Kubernetes native Conjur Follower.

known_issues.title: KI#00025604: CyberArk Identity - Admin Portal StepUp ISPSS for legacy tenants (my.idaptive.app)
known_issues.description: Legacy tenants using my.idaptive.app encounter CSRF errors when performing step-up MFA in the Admin Portal under ISPSS. This issue does not occur with id.cyberark.cloud tenants. Steps: Login to tenant, switch to admin portal, click Continue MFA, see CSRF error. For all admin users requiring step-up MFA for Admin Portal access in ISPSS, the flow fails.
known_issues.workaround: Contact Technical Support and request that they disable StepUp MFA for your tenant's Identity administration portal.

known_issues.title: KI#00025458: EPM Win agent - A large amount of events are being created and displayed on EPM console
KI#00025623: Unix - CP upgrade fail
known_issues.description: When following the documentation for CP upgrade on Unix, the /etc/opt/CARKaim/vault/vault.ini file is empty after the upgrade/repair. The upgrade fails with error: CASPR001E Error parsing parameters file, Reason: Parms file [/etc/opt/CARKaim/vault/vault.ini] line [0]: Section []: Mandatory parameter <Vault> is missing.
known_issues.workaround: 1. Back up /etc/opt/CARKaim/vault/vault.ini before upgrade, e.g. to /var/tmp/vault.ini. 2. Change the vault path in aimparms to /var/tmp/vault.ini instead of the documentation path. 3. Run the Upgrade/Repair.

known_issues.title: KI#00012026: Vault LDAP sync process deletes all LDAP users if LDAPConf.xml fails to load correctly
known_issues.description: If the LDAPConf.xml fails to load correctly for any reason, the LDAP users will be deleted the next time LDAP sync runs on the Vault server. It's possible LDAPConf.xml could be corrupted as part of storage corruption in a Cluster Vault, as part of a failing replication, or XML syntax error (in the event XML file was directly edited). Some symptoms in the italog.log: ITALD015E LDAP configuration was not refreshed successfully. ITALD007W LDAP module is not configured properly. (Reason: ITALD014E Error loading LDAP configuration file (Reason: Error decrypting external file). ) Note: The reason for ITALD007W can be different Soon after these errors, you'll see the users get deleted: ITATS690I LDAP synchronization process, deleting external group Secure Admins. Reason: 5
known_issues.workaround: If error has already occurred, you may restore from a DR or PAReplicate backup. If backup is not available, users/groups must be added back to their applicable safes manually. To prevent the bug from deleting users/groups, you may also set ExternalObjectsUpdatePolicy in the DBParm.ini to the following: ExternalObjectsUpdatePolicy=DeleteNone This prevents any users from being removed from the Vault automatically.

known_issues.title: KI#00025677: EPM not communicating on 26.3
known_issues.description: When the EPM agent is installed or upgraded via MDM, the agent may fail to communicate with the EPM console after deployment. This behavior appears to be specific to upgrades or installations initiated through MDM. Recommended to perform agent upgrades directly from the EPM Console to avoid the communication issue. A fix is planned for the upcoming EPM 26.3 release.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00025524: Wrong SELinux labels for new directories when ServerHostKeyNotInCacheBehavior=4
known_issues.description: If initial selinux attributes on PSMShadowUser home directory were changed, restorecon will not restore to correct selinux attributes. Reproduction steps: Set ServerHostKeyNotInCacheBehavior=4, Selinux enforce, restorecon -RFvv /home/PSMShadowUser, attempt to connect (via PSMP) to a target host whose SSH host key has changed.
known_issues.workaround: Manually restore selinux attributes on PSMShadowUser home directory: chcon -R -t home_root_t /home/PSMShadowUser/.ssh /home/PSMShadowUser/errors

known_issues.title: KI#00025664: CyberArk Identity - Using an Entra Directory Service with Additional MFA will Redirect Users to UserPortal Following Authentication
known_issues.description: When using an Entra Directory Service with a second MFA challenge enabled in the Authentication Policy, once the User completes both authentication challenges they are redirected to the Identity UserPortal instead of the PIN page or Authentication Successful page for SIA. Root cause: the way the back-end code handles additional MFA processes for Entra Directory Users.

known_issues.title: KI#00025483: 32bit Connection Component were used by 64bit WebAppDispatcher
known_issues.description: The PSM connection component were originally created and tested on a 32-bit architecture. Recent updates to PSM changed how {PSMComponentsFolder} behaves. PSM now attempts to run these connectors using a 64-bit architecture first, but the connectors still contain 32-bit preconnect DLLs. If a target WebApp does not support a 64-bit dispatcher, the system reverts to a 32-bit dispatcher. Error: PSMDU018E Dispatcher error: System.BadImageFormatException: Could not load file or assembly - An attempt was made to load a program with an incorrect format.
known_issues.workaround: Change the paths manually: From "{PSMComponentsFolder}\CyberArk.PSM.WebAppDispatcher.exe" "{PSMComponentsFolder}" To "{PSMComponentsFolder32}\CyberArk.PSM.WebAppDispatcher.exe" "{PSMComponentsFolder32}"

known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00018515: Conjur becomes unhealthy when etcd is intermittently unhealthy
known_issues.description: When etcd is intermittently unhealthy, it causes two problems: * The Conjur logs contain the following pattern at occasional intervals: {code:java} ... evoke-cluster - ... WARN -- : state is now unhealthy ... evoke-cluster - ... INFO -- : dap-master.company.com is not healthy ... evoke-cluster - ... WARN -- : state is now running ... evoke-cluster - ..INFO -- : dap-master.company.com acting as master {code} * The Conjur node becomes unhealthy, which can lead to load balancers no longer routing requests to the active Leader. The underlying cause for etcd becoming unhealthy is that the system's disk I/O performance (as measured by fsync durations) temporarily falls beneath the threshold that etcd considers healthy. This can happen for a number of reasons, for example: * The underlying disk performance is below the system requirements for etcd. * There is contention for the available disk I/O, for example between etcd and the Postgresql database server.
known_issues.workaround: It is possible to mitigate the frequency of Conjur health issues caused by etcd by maximizing the disk IO performance of the Conjur servers. Possible options for improving this include: * Documenting the auto-failover system requirements to meet or exceed the requirements for etcd. * Reducing the contention for disk I/O. For example, by tuning the database and etcd disk usage or using separate filesystems for etcd and the Conjur database. * Reduce the impact of intermittent etcd health failures on the overall Conjur health.

known_issues.title: KI#00025666: Domain PSM users replaced with local account credentials when clean installation perfomed on the same host
known_issues.description: When a PSM server is clean-installed a second time on the same host (e.g., after an OS rebuild), the installation registration overwrites the existing PSMConnect/PSMAdminConnect Objects with local-user credentials, overwriting any domain user configuration. If shared between multiple PSMs, all PSMs will retrieve wrong PSMConnect user details and fail to initiate sessions.
known_issues.workaround: Delete the PSMServer node from the configuration or remove the PSMConnect/PSMAdminConnect Objects from the PSM server node before starting a clean installation. If already overwritten, manually update these objects in the PSM safe with the correct username, address and logon domain. It may also be necessary to reset the passwords.

known_issues.title: KI#00024116: Double-clicking "Continue" in the Validating security layers prompt results with various unexpected behavior
KI#00020827: Live Session monitoring doesn't work caused by large value in file categories
known_issues.description: The live session monitoring page doesn't load when trying to monitor live sessions in PVWA. The following error is shown: "ITAGN001S System error (Code: 601). This error might be due to incompatible client version. Contact your system administrator." After investigation we found that the error is caused by huge numbers in file categories of files in the PSMLiveSessions or PSMPLiveSessions safe in the Vault. This can be confirmed in PrivateArk Client by opening the PSMLiveSessions/PSMPLiveSessions safe. Right click on one of the files and select Properties -> File Categories. If there is a very large number (10 digits or more) in one of the File Category values, then this bug is present.
known_issues.workaround: For each PSM/PSMP server do the following steps: 1. Stop the "CyberArk Privileged Session Manager" service or PSMP service. 2. log in to PrivateArk Client as an Administrator user (will require permissions on the PSMLiveSessions/PSMPLiveSessions safe). 3. Open and step inside the PSMLiveSessions/PSMPLiveSessions safe. 4. Right-click and delete <PSM_Server_ID>.LiveSessions file 5. Start the "CyberArk Privileged Session Manager" / PSMP service. 6. Log off PrivateArk Client. Repeat the above steps for each PSM/PSMP server. Each PSM/PSMP will have have an individual live sessions file.

known_issues.title: KI#00023569: Suspend/Terminate API does not work with Distributed Vaults
KI#00025523: SecretsSyncServ TX triggers Audit codes 0 and 1
known_issues.description: The SecretsSyncServ TX triggers Audit codes 0 and 1. If the customer is monitoring on either of these audit codes they will receive a syslog notification and they will also appear in the PVWA Activity Report. These audit codes don't make sense for the transaction. 0 = Delete Directory Map (Unauthorized), 1 = Delete Directory Map. Reproducible when re-running the Modern secret sync process.
known_issues.workaround: Remove audit codes 0 and 1 from the syslog message code setup. When running the PVWA Activity report, don't report on 0 = Delete Directory Map (Unauthorized) and 1 = Delete Directory Map. These aren't relevant in Priv Cloud Shared (ISPSS).

known_issues.title: KI#00025596: CyberArk Identity - Concurrent Sessions do not work for Federated accounts (no additonal MFA)
known_issues.description: Configure Maximum concurrent sessions allowed to 1 in the policy. However, federated users are still able to log in from multiple locations (mobile/another browser). If a federated user does additional MFA, concurrent sessions are tracked. If not, concurrent sessions are not tracked. Root cause: Concurrent MFA session checks are missing in Federation AuthCookie.TryParseSaml and AuthCookie.TryParseOidc.
known_issues.workaround: Apply Additional MFA for federated accounts.

known_issues.title: KI#00024954: CyberArk Identity - “Keep me signed in” option in New Navigation Experience does not maintain the session after browser restart when using IBE
known_issues.description: When users enable the "Keep me signed in" option in policy and sign in to the user portal through the Identity Browser Extension (IBE), the session does not persist after restarting the browser. This occurs when the tenant uses the new navigation experience for the Identity User Portal. Steps to Reproduce: Enable the Keep me signed in option in the policy. Sign in to the user portal and access IBE. Restart the browser. Observe that IBE does not automatically sign in again after a restart. Expected Result: When Keep me signed in is enabled, IBE should automatically sign in after the browser restarts.
known_issues.workaround: After restarting the browser, open the user portal once. This will trigger IBE to automatically sign in.

known_issues.title: KI#00024844: Multiple PSMApp User Session Contention
known_issues.description: PSMApp user triggers 2 sessions that compete for the same resources and many transactions will be waiting for the locks of the resources. If other PSMs are restarted at the same time, it will trigger the same type of transactions that due to a deadlock will never finish and they will time out with the following error: ----- ITADM112E Database Error (Lock wait timeout exceeded; try restarting transaction). ----- On high loaded vaults this will cause a performance degradation that is growing over time and cause the vault to be unresponsive.
known_issues.workaround: As a workaround: - Do not restart many PSM servers at the same time i.e. restart one PSM every 15minutes - Monitor long running transactions to terminate PSM transactions after 30 seconds: MonitorLongTransactions=U"PSM",15,29,30 - Comment the ApiKeyPath parameter in PSM's vault.ini file if architecture is not Distributed Vaults (ExclusiveUnlockAfterPSMSession enabled) or Ticketing System Integration is enabled. If Ticketing System Integration is enabled, rolling back this integration will avoid the issue.

known_issues.title: KI#00024976: PSMConnect object retrieved multiple times during RDP file generation

Frequency: Always. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Log in to PVWA as a user with permissions to initiate PSM sessions. 2. Navigate to a target system and initiate a connection using RDP by clicking Connect button. 3. Check Vault logs to confirm the object retrieval. Expected Results: At this point of connection, PSMConnect object retrieval is unnecessary.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00025242: CyberArk Identity - New Password Reset Screen Does Not Work for Non-English Languages.
known_issues.description: When the browser language is changed to a language other than English, the new password reset screen does not behave dynamically. The password requirements are not validated in real time. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Change the browser language to a non-English language. 2. Attempt to reset the password for a user. 3. Observe that when typing the new password, the requirements are not dynamically updated. Expected Results: As the user types, each requirement should be validated dynamically (turning from red to green). Actual Results: Password requirements remain static and do not reflect progress when using non-English languages.
known_issues.workaround: Disable the new password reset screen to restore functionality.

known_issues.title: KI#00023698: Master can't logon from the Vault machine without EmergencyStationIP paramater
known_issues.description: Since adding IPv6 support (14.2) an empty client IP address is sent for the Master account logon when done locally from the Vault. This happens if the EmergencyStationIP parameter is not specified. How to reproduce: Point on RecoveryPrvKey in dbparm.ini, install GUI Client on the Vault, set ServerAddress=127.0.0.1, logon with user Master. Error: ITATS008E User master cannot be used from IP Address. Same error occurs when using Vault_IP or PACLI. The empty IP address causes Master logon to fail.
known_issues.workaround: Specify the local vault address along with EmergencyStationIP in the dbparm.ini when logging in as Master locally on the Vault. Example: EmergencyStationIP=Enter.Vault.IP.Here. Requires PrivateArk Vault Server service restart to take effect.

The error was printed even though there was no real issue with the sequence.

known_issues.title: KI#00025509: PSM: Slow performance when running PSM /livesessions API call
known_issues.description: When requesting the Live Sessions via V10 Monitoring UI or RestAPI call, this can result in long wait time for the returned data. This is caused by an Inefficient Query Pattern resulting in repeated transactions to occur. When performed in larger environment, the number of queries generated increases adding additional time for each query and delaying the process.
known_issues.workaround: Issue is not seen when using the Classic UI. Reducing the limit value in the API call also helps.

known_issues.title: KI#00025323: [SF 04147641] Rebaser's leader health check should verify postgres connectivity
known_issues.description: Rebaser's leader health check should verify postgres connectivity
known_issues.workaround: If the leader's health wouldn't respond to follower the rebaser would keep on waiting but keep health status ok. Possibly disabling leader's LB for the time of the upgrade would help to prevent premature rebase process.

known_issues.title: KI#00021377: PSMP server does not work if incorrect TunnelingServerPort value is set
known_issues.description: General description: TunnelingServerPort should contain only one port number. In case user updates this value (in PVWA or basic_psmpserver.conf) with multiple entries or other value that cannot be converted to proper port number, psmpserver crashes. Steps to reproduce: For TunnelingServerPort value set value other than 16 bit integer i.e.: "55555,23", "", "invalid" Restart psmpsrv service.
known_issues.workaround: To resolve the issue either: 1) Change the value of TunnelingServerPort to a correct value (integer representing a valid port number). 2) In case there is no plan to use tunneling feature: Remove the parameter completely. You can find the value in PVWA's global configuration section: Enter Administration -> Options, then Connection Components -> PSMP-SSH -> Components Parameters -> TunnelingServerPort To remove the parameter right click on its name and in context menu choose "Delete". After performing one of the above, next restart of the psmpsrv service should continue normally.

KI#00017002: Mutex Error - System.Threading.AbandonedMutexException: The wait completed due to an abandoned mutex.
known_issues.description: Running a large number of TPC instances (for example 1,000) results in a failed to acquire mutex and TPC failed to run action. Error Messages: System.Threading.AbandonedMutexException: The wait completed due to an abandoned mutex. Output in logs: Info -> MutexHandler :: AcquireMutex -> Start 08.07.2021 01:12:32.224 | Info -> MutexHandler :: GetMutex -> Start 08.07.2021 01:12:32.224 | Info -> MutexHandler :: GetExistingMutex -> Trying to open 'HeavyCodeSection' mutex 08.07.2021 01:12:32.224 | Info -> MutexHandler :: GetExistingMutex -> Trying to get 'HeavyCodeSection' mutex ownership. Timeout interval: 120000 08.07.2021 01:13:03.435 | ERROR -> b1 :: a -> General error occurred. Refer to log for more information 08.07.2021 01:13:03.451 | ERROR -> b1 :: a -> System.Threading.AbandonedMutexException: The wait completed due to an abandoned mutex. at System.Threading.WaitHandle.InternalWaitOne(SafeHandle waitableSafeHandle, Int64 millisecondsTimeout, Boolean hasThreadAffinity, Boolean exitContext) at System.Threading.WaitHandle.WaitOne(Int32 millisecondsTimeout, Boolean exitContext) at System.Threading.WaitHandle.WaitOne(Int32 millisecondsTimeout) at CyberArk.Extensions.Utilties.MutexHandler.GetExistingMutex(String validMutexName, Int32 validTimeoutInterval) at CyberArk.Extensions.Utilties.MutexHandler.GetMutex(String validMutexName, Int32 validTimeoutInterval, Boolean isRetry) at CyberArk.Extensions.Utilties.MutexHandler.AcquireMutex(String mutexName, Int32 timeoutInterval)
known_issues.workaround: Change LockInitAndSpawn to false. This value is set in: C:\Program Files (x86)\CyberArk\Password Manager\bin\CyberArk.TPC.exe.config

KI#00017460: Credential provider upgrade/repair failure when all licenses used (Win or Unix)
known_issues.description: When an upgrade (or repair) is performed on a CP machine (there are 2 in total in this example environment, using 2 out of 2 AppProvider users in the Vault license), the upgrade fails with the following error in the CreateEnv log (which is where the installer attempts to update the CP env within the Vault): [05/10/2021 19:36:11] :: ITATS937E Limit of 2 licensed AppProvider users exceeded. New user will not be added. (Vault [CAMainVault] user [Administrator]) [05/10/2021 19:36:11] :: Error occurred while trying to create Users. Reason: ITATS937E Limit of 2 licensed AppProvider users exceeded. New user will not be added. Cause: The upgrade procedure attempts to use an extra AppProvider user during the upgrade procedure, which is not possible when all AppProvider users are already being utilized.
known_issues.workaround: - During the upgrade procedure, skip the step whereby the CP attempts to connect to the Vault (for example, by clicking "No"). The upgrade of the CP environment in the Vault can be performed outside of the upgrade wizard by running "CreateEnv" as an Administrator. The upgrade of the CP machine itself should be complete up to this step. - Obtain a temporary license that contains more AppProvider users to assist in the CP upgrade

known_issues.title: KI#00024828: Wrong SIA rdp filename when downloaded from pCloud
known_issues.description: When downloading a SIA RDP file for a domain account, the generated filename includes the domain name twice and omits the actual target machine name. Although this does not impact the connection itself, the filename is used to identify the session. As a result, users working with multiple simultaneous connections may find it difficult to distinguish between targets.
known_issues.workaround: The user can rename the downloaded rdp file.

known_issues.title: KI#00021361: Filtering for Client name does not work in PVWA / Privileged Sessions / Monitoring
known_issues.description: In PVWA | Privileged Sessions | Monitoring menu filtering for "Client" property does not work, it always gives zero results, both on v10 and v9 GUI. The "Client" property is actually the display name of the connection component that was used for the PSM connection. Let's assume we have a connection component with display name "GAIA" (ID=PSM-GAIA). Searching for "GAIA" or "PSM-GAIA" in the session property filter gives zero results, while without using any session property filter the search brings up the recording that has "GAIA" in the Client property field. This is not version specific issue, we can observe the same behavior on v12.6.7 and on v13.2 PVWA as well.
known_issues.workaround: Using one of the raw properties of the recorded session, you will need to add a property on the following: Administration -> Options -> Priviledged Session Management -> General Settings -> Search Properties -> Recording Name of new property = ConnectionComponentID For the partial search of name, you will also need to set "OptimizeRecordingsSearch" to "No"

known_issues.title: KI#00025488: Session monitoring not updated on early connection failure.

This results in a session not being marked as failed, causing customers to worry it is still operating when in fact it crashed. This impacts the visibility of sessions and appears rare, but does not impact connectivity itself.

known_issues.title: KI#00025241: Reconnect Flow Reuses Cached Ephemeral User Without Applying Updated Policy Groups (ZSP Local)
known_issues.description: When a user connects via ZSP Local flow with Reconnect enabled, the ephemeral user created during the first session is cached. If the user's policy changes during the reconnect window (e.g., from Standard to Admin), the next connection reuses the cached ephemeral user without applying updated group assignments from the new policy. Impact: The Session diagnostics UI shows updated groups from the Access Authorization Service, but they are not actually applied because the provisioning service skips group updates for cached ephemeral users. The UI implies a group update occurred, but the target machine still shows the old group membership.

KI#00024675: Hours Until Session Expire parameter causes "Service is Unavailable" error
known_issues.description: The CyberArk Mobile app expires user credentials based on the Session Parameters - Hours Until Expire setting in CyberArk Identity. This causes issues with offline credential synchronization, as users must re-enter their credentials every time this period elapses. The maximum setting is 48 hours, requiring users to re-authenticate every 2 days to maintain offline sync functionality. Steps to reproduce: 1. Set "Hours Until Session Expire" to 1 hour in Identity under the policy that test user is part of. 2. Wait 1 hour and open the CyberArk mobile app - "Service is Unavailable" error appears. 3. Enter credentials under the tab in mobile. 4. Credentials will be valid again for another hour.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00025452: Vault-HSM Disconnection when running CAVaultManager CollectLogs
known_issues.description: Vault outage occurs if ReconnectHSMOnErrorCodes is not configured properly when vault administrator executes CAVaultManager CollectLogs. Steps: Install Vault and Configure HSM Integration, execute CAVaultManager CollectLogs, attempt to connect to an account/monitor logs. Decryption-related errors appear: ITACM027S Communication error (Diagnostic information: 527) and ITATS103E Safe key is incorrect.
known_issues.workaround: Configure ReconnectHSMOnErrorCodes to include the HSM error code. For example, ReconnectHSMOnErrorCodes=220,224 from the error (reproduced with CRYPT(1,2) logging enabled): C_Decrypt failed, Rv:220 and Rv:224.

known_issues.workaround: Use the EVD Files List Report.

known_issues.title: KI#00023874: [PAM on Cloud] - AWS/ PSM: NLA enabled despite meeting prerequisites

known_issues.workaround: 1. Open the Run dialog box. 2. Type sysdm.cpl and press Enter to open the System Properties window. 3. In the System Properties window, go to the Remote tab. 4. Under the Remote Desktop section, you will see the option Allow connections only from computers running Remote Desktop with Network Level Authentication (recommended). 5. Uncheck this option.

known_issues.title: KI#00023044: CPM - CPM will disable inactive account in Dual Accounts setup when verify
known_issues.description: General description: When Dual Accounts are set up and a verify on an inactive account is performed, it will fail and the account will be disabled in the CPM when max retries will be reached. Steps to reproduce: Setup Dual Accounts and perform Verify on the inactive account until Max Retries are reached. Current Results: CPM disables management on the inactive account. Expected Results: CPM does not disable inactive account. Error Messages: CACPM485E CPM doesn't support a member of rotational group that is not the active one.
known_issues.workaround: Disable verify action on platform.

KI#00025345: Case 04152697 - Not clear message in the log
known_issues.description: When EPM has certain policies present, the Linux EPM agent may try to modify some files in /etc/pam.d to make the EPM policies work properly (/etc/nsswitch.conf, /etc/ssh/sshd_config, /etc/pam.d/su, /etc/pam.d/sshd, /etc/pam.d/sudo). Customer creates a Linux Identity bridge policy which would trigger EPM to modify /etc/pam.d/sshd. If there is bad syntax in other files such as /etc/pam.d/password-auth or /etc/pam.d/system-auth, then EPM might fail to modify /etc/pam.d/sshd which would prevent the intended policies from working. Troubleshooting is difficult because EPM might not add a log entry indicating it couldn't make the necessary file updates.
known_issues.workaround: Fix the configuration file with bad syntax.

known_issues.title: KI#00018066: Accounts Dicovery - Schedule discovery task still triggered by scanner at scheduled time after deleting it on the PVWA
known_issues.description: After deleting a scheduled discovery from the PVWA , the scanner still tries to trigger the scan and fails. In the CACPMScanner.log: 14/12/2021 13:50:00.309 | tid=20 | user=CACPMScanner | Request failed, Error Code: CAWS00001E. Reason: System.Exception: The discovery has been deleted. at CyberArk.PasswordVault.API.NG.WF.DiscoveryServicesContainer.GetDiscoveryTask(Int32 BLTaskID, String BLTaskName) at ajn.a(Int32 A_0). PVWA logs 2021-12-14 13:50:00,294 ERROR [29] <TaskGUID> EPVWA112E Failed to get discovery task for update. Error: [System.Exception: The discovery has been deleted. at CyberArk.PasswordVault.API.NG.WF.DiscoveryServicesContainer.GetDiscoveryTask(Int32 BLTaskID, String BLTaskName) at ajn.a(Int32 A_0)] [ajn] 2021-12-14 13:50:00,294 DEBUG [29] <TaskGUID> aqy: System.Exception: The discovery has been deleted. at CyberArk.PasswordVault.API.NG.WF.DiscoveryServicesContainer.GetDiscoveryTask(Int32 BLTaskID, String BLTaskName) at ajn.a(Int32 A_0) at ajn.a(Int32 A_0)
known_issues.workaround: Restart the scanner service.

known_issues.title: KI#00025313: Improper Group Removal Validation Causing False Failures During AD Provisioning
known_issues.description: During AD Provisioning, failures are shown in the job reports suggesting that the provisioning process failed. Despite this, Users are successfully provisioned and updated. This also applies to incremental sync jobs. The cause of this bug is because the Group Removal stage of the provisioning flow presents a failure due to improper validation which results in the provisioning job to present a full failure incorrectly. The failure may appear with status Failed, Sub status UnexpectedUserSyncException and error "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" even though changes have been made successfully in Active Directory.
known_issues.workaround: There are no known workarounds for this issue at this time, however it is important to note that this bug does not appear to affect the sync of data for Users at this time.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Enable DoDBanner: System Configuration -> Options -> General: ShowDoDBanner=Yes. 2. Enable and set the PKIPN authentication method as the default: System Configuration -> Options -> Authentication Methods -> Add AuthMethod: Id=pkipn Enabled=Yes; System Configuration -> Options -> Authentication Method -> GeneralSettings DefaultMethod=pkipn. 3. Logout from PVWA system. 4. Reopen/Refresh the PVWA logon page: /PasswordVault/v10/logon -> automatically redirected to /PasswordVault/v10/logon/pkipn. Current Results: The PKIPN logon action is triggered before clicking "I agree" on the DoD Notice and Consent Banner. Expected Results: The PKIPN logon action should be started after clicking "I agree" on the DoD Notice and Consent Banner.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00025173: PSM recovery procedure behavior when all Vaults are not available
known_issues.description: PSM errors out with 'The server could not complete the operation because you exceeded the maximum amount of concurrent transactions'. End-User Impact: If session relogon fails to all available Vaults with communication errors then the behavior may lead to cause Primary Vault being inoperable because of countless logon transactions. Affected Scope: Potentially all components depending on Vault operability may become temporarily inoperable for all customers using multiple Vault IPs. Frequency: Rarely. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Environment with PSM and Vault >=2 count. 2. All Vaults become inoperable. 3. Recovery procedure on PSM starts automatically, retries logon to primary vault, switches over after timeout, and after checking all Vaults switches back to main Vault sending logon transactions until succeeded. Root Cause: Most probably because most PSM Vault sessions are not using SessionAllowSwitchVaultAddress flag.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

If the GroupName file category contains only a space character the report will fail with the error: "CASVL008E Object name [ ] is invalid" in the log.
known_issues.workaround: Remove the GroupName file category from the problematic account. Reach out to CyberArk support to find which account/s is causing the issue.

known_issues.title: KI#00024853: DownloadCARevocationDataServ transaction causes Vault Crash
known_issues.description: Customers using PKI or TLS (if the CA-signed Certificate includes CDP) may experience a crash in version 14.6 when the following parameters are configured in the dbparm.ini file: TrustedCAPath CARevocationPath CARevocationDataDownloadInterval CARevocationDistributionPoint This issue arises because the vault is unable to reach the CRL URL, regardless of its validity. Instead of crashing, the expected behavior should be an error message from the vault.
known_issues.workaround: Removing the following from dbparm.ini: CARevocationDataDownloadInterval CARevocationDistributionPoint

known_issues.title: KI#00016777: Calling to the Same Logic Container Command Multiple Times at the Same Time Results in Error ITALC005E Preventing the Creation of Platforms and Onboarding of Accounts in the Existing Platform.
known_issues.description: If two threads execute the same command at the same time, it can create a state corruption on the LC which may prevent any further commands from running. As a result it prevents the creation of platforms and onboarding of accounts in the existing platform. Issue is caused by updating a non-thread safe resource from multiple threads. Can also apply to different commands with the same parameters type. Command must not have been called previously before being called concurrently Error info: 25/04/2021 23:00:15.680 | tid=12 | user=CPMPROD01 | RunService | Exception Caught | Message: ITALC005E, Casting exception - unable to cast RunBLServiceParams to ExecuteMethodParams. 25/04/2021 23:00:15.682 | tid=12 | user=CPMPROD01 | RunService | Exception Caught | InnerException: System.IndexOutOfRangeException: Index was outside the bounds of the array. at System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary`2.Insert(TKey key, TValue value, Boolean add) at CyberArk.AppServices.LogicContainer.Runtime.SerializationServices.SaveTypeInLoadedTypesMap(String objectTypeNameSpace, String objectTypeName, Type objectType) at CyberArk.AppServices.LogicContainer.Runtime.SerializationServices.FindType(String objectTypeName, String objectTypeNameSpace) at CyberArk.AppServices.LogicContainer.Runtime.SerializationServices.CreateTypeByFullName(String objectTypeName, String objectTypeNameSpace) at CyberArk.AppServices.LogicContainer.Runtime.SerializationServices.DeserializeObject(String objValueToDeserialize, List`1 knownTypes) at CyberArk.AppServices.LogicContainer.Runtime.RunBLServiceParams.op_Implicit(RunBLServiceParams prms) at InfraBLService.BLService.RunService(RunBLServiceParams details)
known_issues.workaround: Behavior is very specific and therefore shouldn't occur too often

known_issues.title: KI#00025198: P023 Can Timeout In Large Environments
known_issues.description: RUN_DIAGNOSTICS P023 can fail and all other parts of PTA work fine, in an environment with many many safes. P023 performs a logon, then get all safes to verify the token from the logon. P023 has a 15 second timeout before it auto-fails. In environments with thousands of safes, this get all safes will take too long (no matter how fast the environment itself is) and timeout the call, showing a FAIL response in RUN_DIAGNOSTICS.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00022040: Groups with "/" in the name created with option "AppendFriendlyDomainNameToGroup=Yes"
known_issues.description: General description: While using parameter "AppendFriendlyDomainNameToGroup=Yes" groups in the Vault are created with "/". There are problems related to that and there is no supported way to update them programmatically the safe ownership (API) Steps to reproduce: Create LDAP integration and set "AppendFriendlyDomainNameToGroup" parameter to "Yes". Current Results: 1. If we try to manage permissions of such group with '/' character via PWVA we will get following pop-up window on applying new configuration. 2. Its possible to create a group with '/' character in the Vault using PrivateArk Client, but when such group is added as a member of a Safe it ends up in a following error: 3. Using existing REST APIs that are using the "MemberName" to refer to the user/group is not possible: Update Safe member Delete Safe member Get Safe member Since "/" is a forbidden character and there is no way to escape it, customer cannot use REST API to update these members. Expected Results: 1. Usually when trying to apply new configuration while managing permissions it should succeed with following notification at the top of the page. 2. The name is not violating Vault specifications therefore it should update successfully. 3. It should be possible to perform these operations (i.e. by using the MemberID instead of MemberName, as it is used for Update group or Update user commands OR by making it not possible to use not supported characters within the group name) Error Messages: Logs: n/a Other Symptoms: n/a Tenant ID / Pod Number: n/a
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00025155: PSMP - Reason text for connection cannot contain non-English characters
known_issues.description: When using PSMP connections, if the specified access reason contains any non-ASCII characters, such as umlauts or letters with diacritics, the connection will fail. Steps to reproduce: 1. Configure sessions to require specifying the reason for access. 2. Connect to target via PSMP. 3. Provide valid vault credentials. 4. In the access reason form, enter a string containing non-ASCII characters. 5. Proceed with the connection. Current Results: The login will fail with error: ITATS012E Invalid parameters. Details: CVTPT114E Input parameter [OperationReason] I18N string conversion has failed. Reason: CAS8N202E ICU conversion from utf-8 to UTF16 failed. Expected Results: The session is successfully established.
known_issues.workaround: Just use English characters for the reason

known_issues.description: DNA Tool fails to scan AWS IAM users. Steps to reproduce: Create an IAM user with access key on AWS with required authorizations. On DNA tool start an AWS scan, select AWS option, enter access id and secret, select AWS IAM users. Result: scan fails with error: scan initialization failed. known_issues.title: KI#00024556: [UI] [Accounts] Password change complexity criteria inconsistency
known_issues.description: Set of characters being used for verification of special characters complexity criteria is different in frontend than in the backend. There is a need to align it to the same set as used by CASOS/CPM which is: /*!$%()=?{[]}\+#-.,<'_:;>~|@. Steps to reproduce: 1. Add account for a platform with settings: PasswordLength=8, MinUpperCase=1, MinLowerCase=1, MinDigit=1, MinSpecial=1. 2. Navigate to added account. 3. Use 'Change' button and select "Change the password only in the vault." 4. Provide password using only one special character like ^, & or " (complexity criteria appears met as condition is marked green) e.g. Pas&s123. 5. Press 'Change' to confirm. Current Results: Message "The password typed does not adhere to current password policy rules" appears. Password is not changed. Expected Results: Change button should not be active as criteria is not met. Error: CASVL025E This password does not meet the policy requirements - not enough special characters.
known_issues.workaround: Special characters out of set defined in CASOS can usually be used in passwords, they are just not considered as special characters by the backend. Make sure you meet MinSpecial criteria using characters from this set: /*!$%()=?{[]}\+#-.,<'_:;>~|@

KI#00024995: CyberArk SWS - AWS sessions are not recorded when multi-session support is enabled
known_issues.description: When using multi-session capability within AWS not all sessions are captured by Secure Web Sessions. In order to differentiate between sessions on Cloud Service Providers, SWS attempts to detect the logged-in user in each browser tab. For AWS, this was previously done by tracking a cookie. However, when enabling the new Multi-Session Support, AWS replaces the cookie mechanism with a different method.
known_issues.workaround: As an administrator, configure a Session Control rule to disable the "Enable Multi-Session Support" option in the user dialog. Ensure that this rule is applied across all application permissions.

KI#00024932: SWS is unable to record sessions for a specific Azure subdomain
known_issues.description: End-User Impact: Admin/Auditor won't see any recording events for this subdomain. Frequency: sometimes. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Click on the Azure application in the Identity user portal 2. The user will be redirected to the Azure portal, and the extension icon will be marked with a red circle 3. Navigate to a different subdomain / open a new tab and navigate to a different subdomain such as security.microsoft.com. Current Results: The extension icon won't be marked with a red circle and the policy won't be enforced. Root Cause: SWS classifies sessions for the cloud console based on both the domain and the logged-in username. To identify the username for each tab and subdomain, the SWS extension intercepts network calls and attempts to extract metadata related to the authenticated user. For these subdomains, the extension is unable to retrieve the logged-in username.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00025052: Update Python SDK examples for Secrets Manager SaaS
known_issues.description: The docs describing the Python SDK usage are geared towards username/password authentication which isn't supported in Secrets Manager SaaS. Changes needed: Under 'Step 2: Define connection parameters': Change references to username to HOST_ID/host_id, change references of password to API_KEY/api_key. Under 'Step 4: Create the credentials provider': Update code example to set username from host_id, and api_key from api_key. Under 'Use the client': Remove the section 'Log in to the client' (unnecessary, the client authenticates each request as needed). Under 'Supported client methods': Reword the description.
known_issues.workaround: Use api_key credential and authenticate() instead of password credential and login() as documented

known_issues.title: KI#00023585: SSL error after PSMP reaches 365 days uptime
known_issues.description: After PSM for SSH service runs for longer than 365 days it is no longer possible to establish ssh connection. Client sees following error: Handshake failed with fatal error SSL_ERROR_SSL: error:14090086:SSL routines:ssl3_get_server_certificate:certificate verify failed. Steps to Reproduce: Allow PSMP service to run for than 365 days. Current Results: Sessions failed to be established. Expected Results: Sessions gets established without issues.
known_issues.workaround: Restart the PSMP service before 365 days have passed.

known_issues.title: KI#00018512: PSMP crashes upon high amount of local recordings
known_issues.description: When there are a high amount of recordings on the PSMP, on some occasions the PSMP fails to run properly
known_issues.workaround: PSMP functionality can be returned, by moving the local (psmp) recordings aside to another location. ie Move the files within, /var/opt/CARKpsmp/recordings to another location on the PSMP. Please review options and steps for dealing with these recordings with CyberArk Support. - Manually upload recordings to the Vault Recordings Safe. - Allow PSMP to attempt uploading recordings in smaller batches.

known_issues.title: KI#00023673: ClientApp treats pubkeys with rsa-sha2-256/512 algorithms as deprecated ssh-rsa
known_issues.description: Since OpenSSH 8.8, usage of SSH public keys of ssh-rsa type using ssh-rsa (SHA1) algorithm were disabled by default. When used in conjunction with OpenSSH servers with disabled ssh-rsa algorithm, PSMP fails to allow ssh-rsa key type usage even when a target machine and PSM for SSH client can communicate using newer, still supported, algorithms for ssh-rsa key type: rsa-sha2-256 and rsa-sha2-512.
known_issues.workaround: Add at the end of /etc/ssh/ssh_config on the PSM for SSH machine the following: Match localuser PSMShadowUser,PSMP_* PubkeyAcceptedKeyTypes +ssh-rsa Match all

known_issues.title: KI#00024662: Memory leak with long-living commands with large terminal outputs
known_issues.description: Executing commands generating large amount of output may result in memory leak over longer-lived PSMP sessions.
known_issues.workaround: Switch ClientApp from ssh to plink for PSMP versions still containing it.

KI#00019563: Linux agent fails to register using proxy
KI#00024999: ENE Not Sending Approver Users Account Request Email
known_issues.description: When creating new users, groups and roles for Dual Control safes, the approvers do not get email alerts. End-User Impact: Approvers not getting email alerts on requests or other dual control issues. Affected Scope: Users with higher set privileges do not receive email notifications from ENE as approvers. Frequency: Always, until permissions have been modified again such as removing the user from Privilege Cloud Admins. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Create a DUAL CONTROL platform 2. Create a SAFE 3. Onboard an account to the above safe 4. Create a Requestor (Role: Privilege Cloud Users) and Confirmer user (Role: Privilege Cloud Administrator) 5. Assign permissions 6. Log in and request access - no email notification sent to Confirmer. Root Cause: There is an issue with OwnersList in the new vault version.
known_issues.workaround: In the DBParm.ini there is a new parameter "OwnerListNewVersion". Setting the parameter from yes to no can resolve the issue. A vault service restart is required since the parameter is in the DBParm.ini file. Current Value: OwnerListNewVersion=Yes. Workaround: OwnerListNewVersion=No

known_issues.title: KI#00023900: SIA-RDP - The GUI should delete target sets first successfully before deleting strong account
known_issues.description: Target-sets are deleted concurrently with the strong account. By doing this, if the target-sets API fails to delete the target set, we can have a dangling target set (i.e., one where the secret it is pointing at is invalid). This results in awkward behaviour in the GUI, as we cannot delete the target set without using the REST API (as the GUI is strong-account-centric). We need to ensure that the target sets are successfully deleted before we try to delete the strong account.
known_issues.workaround: Use REST API to delete the target set.

KI#00024921: Allow re-provisioning of Service Broker instances
known_issues.description: There was a behavior change in the recent Service Broker versions that now assumes a Conjur API key will always be present in the service provisioning response. This is not true in the case of re-provisioning a service instance in an existing space where a host role has been created previously, as an API key is only returned by Conjur when a new role is created. This behavior change was introduced in the migration from the legacy Service Broker implementation when it was migrated to the Golang implementation, so we should make it consistent with the old behavior.
known_issues.workaround: It's possible that the application binding in the space will work using the existing service instance if it still exists. Otherwise an immediate workaround would be to manually delete the relevant host in Conjur policy and recreate the service instance using the cf CLI following that.

KI#00024961: Hardening HardenWindowsGroupPolicy.ps1 fails on German OS
known_issues.description: HardenWindowsGroupPolicy.ps1 fails on German OS. Running 'Write-Output y | secedit /configure' fails because the script expects 'Y' as answer for YES but in German OS the letter for YES is 'J'. The secedit prompt in German asks for J/N instead of Y/N, causing the hardening script to fail to configure Group policy.
known_issues.workaround: Update the following line: Write-Output $yesStr | secedit /configure /db "$SDBFile" /cfg "$WindowsSecFile" /overwrite /log "$logName" -> Write-Output $yesStr | secedit /configure /db "$SDBFile" /cfg "$WindowsSecFile" /overwrite /log "$logName" /quiet

known_issues.description: For a non-RDP connection component with Supported Capabilities including: KeystokresAudit KeystokresTextRecording And a Platform for which the "Recorded Users and Groups" is configured to disable video recording for the user. Then during a session for that user, a text file is created but the file is not uploaded to the vault and the logs show the error: [25/12/2014 | 09:13:03] | :: | PSMSC074E [c06e2a8d-c71c-48b4-81c1-ae9492264912] Failed to upload recording file c06e2a8d-c71c-48b4-81c1-ae9492264912.VID.avi. Error: PSMRC038E Failed to finilize record file (More information: [PSMRC028E Failed to finalize record because PSM could not load the AVI header file [C:\Program Files (x86)\CyberArk\PSM\Recordings\tmp-c06e2a8d-c71c-48b4-81c1-ae9492264912.VID.avi.dat]]) [25/12/2014 | 09:13:03] | :: | PSMSC105E The following expected recording file(s) is (are) missing in the actual recordings list of session 'c06e2a8d-c71c-48b4-81c1-ae9492264912': c06e2a8d-c71c-48b4-81c1-ae9492264912.VID.avi
known_issues.workaround: It is currently not possible to have only text recordings for some users and video recordings for other users. Either video recording capability can be disabled for all users, or if using "Recorded Users and Groups" to specify which users to record then sessions for excluded users will not have video or text recordings uploaded. The error in the logs can be ignored. If text recording files are found in the PSM Recordings or Errors folders they can be moved and archived.

known_issues.title: KI#00024130: After upgrading to version 14 account descriptions with Non-English special characters are truncated
KI#00024939: OPMPreloader on Solaris10Sparc crash in 14.2
known_issues.description: End-User Impact: Executing pimsu commands on Solaris10UltraSparc causes the executed commands to crash. Frequency: Always. Steps to Reproduce: Executing all commands with pimsu causes this issue. Example command: pimsu ls. Expected Results: Executing commands with pimsu should not crash. Root Cause: The opmpreloader library, used when executing commands with pimsu, attempted to operate on a map that stores currently opened file descriptors before it was fully initialized. This behavior changed between the 12.6 and 14.2 releases, most likely due to modifications in the OpenSSL implementation. A stack trace investigation revealed a call to OPENSSL_cpuid_setup during the initialization of opmpreloader. That call attempted to execute open(), which was intercepted by the not-yet-initialized opmpreloader. As a result, it tried to save the file descriptor information to an uninitialized map, causing a crash.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00024938: Insufficient permissions in /opt/CARKopmrt dirs on AIX
known_issues.description: End-User Impact: pimsu commands cannot be executed due to permission denied to files in /opt/CARKopmrt directory. Affected Scope: Users without permissions to read and execute inside /opt/CARKopmrt directory. Frequency: Always. Steps to Reproduce: Set a non-default umask (like 027) for the root user, then install OPM on AIX. When non-root users try to use pimsu, the console outputs: Dependent module /opt/CARKopmrt/8/lib/libstdc++.a(libstdc++.so.6) could not be loaded. System error: Permission denied. Expected Results: pimsu command works for all users. Root Cause: If the root user has a non-default umask set, the AIX pre_install.sh script may create directories under /opt/CARKopmrt with insufficient permissions.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00023775: Password Rotation Issue on Windows 11 24H2 with Local Admin Accounts
known_issues.description: We have identified an issue affecting password rotation on Windows 11 machines running the latest 24H2 release, specifically when managing local administrator accounts via the Windows Desktop Local Account platform. The issue did not occur prior to the 24H2 update and results in an Access is Denied error. During the investigation, it was found that Windows 11 24H2 introduces new group policies that impact password change operations. One such policy, Configure SAM change password RPC methods, appears to be the root cause. Testing confirmed that enabling this policy and setting it to Allow all change password RPC methods allows CPM to rotate passwords successfully. In contrast, selecting Allow strong encryption change password RPC methods only results in the same access error. Reproduction Steps: Environment: Windows 11 24H2, Platform: Windows Desktop Local Account, Action: Attempt to rotate password for local admin account, Result: Operation fails with Access is Denied, Expected Behavior: Successful password rotation.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00023924: HTML5GW connection thread hangs in livelock after user disconnection
known_issues.description: The user disconnection handling in guacd sometimes hangs for some random time making the next session with the same shadow user impossible without admin intervention and manually terminating the session from PVWA. It can happen for non-shadow user connections as well but since most of them allow for multiple sessions the user can start another session without issues although the original session is still running and visible from Monitoring page. When the error occurs, the user will see PSMGW0008E while trying to start a new session to the same target utilizing PSM shadow user account. In the logs, messages include tunnel communication timeout in user thread and User is not responding errors.
known_issues.workaround: Whenever user raises he cannot access previously accessed target, an admin has to terminate his previous session from the Monitoring tab or the user needs to wait until the livelock is resolved.

KI#00024892: Hardening HardenWindowsGroupPolicy.ps1 fails on German OS
known_issues.description: HardenWindowsGroupPolicy.ps1 fails on German OS. Running 'Write-Output y | secedit /configure' fails because the script expects 'Y' as answer for YES but in German OS the letter for YES is 'J'. The secedit prompt in German asks for J/N instead of Y/N, causing the hardening script to fail to configure Group policy.
known_issues.workaround: Update the following line: Write-Output $yesStr | secedit /configure /db "$SDBFile" /cfg "$WindowsSecFile" /overwrite /log "$logName" -> Write-Output $yesStr | secedit /configure /db "$SDBFile" /cfg "$WindowsSecFile" /overwrite /log "$logName" /quiet

known_issues.workaround: In the Identity policy you can change the concurrent sessions to be higher value.

KI#00012029: ITAIGM26W Error moving file trace to archive folder. XMoveFile last error code: 32
known_issues.description: Sometimes when when trying to roll to the next trace and move the current log to the archive folder, the handle to trace file is not closed properly. In this case, you might see the following error in the italog.log: ITAIGM26W Error moving file trace to archive folder. XMoveFile last error code: 32 When we implemented the archive mechanism (controlled by the TraceArchiveMaxSize parameter in dbparm.ini), we designed it like this: Always write to the same trace file. When a rotation is needed, move the file to the archive and continue with the same trace. If error occurs during the move process, we will proceed to the next trace file.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

The secedit prompt in German asks for J/N instead of Y/N, causing the hardening script to fail to configure Group policy.

known_issues.title: KI#00024724: [PGU] PSM Web connector does not work with recent chrome/edge
known_issues.description: Plugin Generator Utility-v1.8.0.1347: The latest browsers no longer support the Selenium-IDE extension because of the manifest version 2. Extension loading is being blocked, and the PGU is not able to connect with the Selenium-IDE extension. The latest Selenium Extension (v3.17.2) was released in 2021 and is no longer under development. Version 134 was the last Chrome/Edge version that fully supported extensions with the v2 manifest. Browsers have an automatic update feature that triggers an update after the first run. AutoUpdate can be disabled by changing registry entries only. Older versions are not available to download from official repositories. Steps to reproduce: Make sure installed Chrome/Edge is version higher than v134. Run PGU -> PSM Web Connector -> update connector settings -> start recording. Current Results: Timeout error -> Failed to load a recorder. Expected Results: Recording started correctly.
known_issues.workaround: The only available solution to have PGU fully functional is to use Edge / Chrome Version 134.0 or lower.

known_issues.title: KI#00022750: Add check if the logon account of the reconcile account is a password or a key
known_issues.description: Currently we are checking only if logon account is password account. but as a logon account it could be also a ssh key account. If logon account is key account then current prompt doesnt work - we expect a password prompt but we log in with key so password prompt is not necessary and we expect standard prompt instead of password prompt in Prereconcile Phase.
known_issues.workaround: There is a workaround. Customers have to add in process.ini file those lines PRLoginExtraUser, InvalidKey, ExtraPassFailInvalidKey PRLoginExtraUser, Password, PRLoginExtraPass PRLoginExtraUser, StandardPrompt, PRSwitchUser instead of : PRLoginExtraUser, Password, PRLoginExtraPass in Prereconcile password phase.

known_issues.title: KI#00024873: When running the registration step with a vault user that has used Ad-Hoc connection, the step fails
known_issues.description: When using the Secure Connect Feature in PVWA, the user launching Secure Connect gets added as a Safe Owner without any rights to the PSMUnmanagedSessionAccount Safe by PVWA. The lack of permissions to update the PSMUnmanagedSessionAccount Safe is causing the upgrade/install to fail. Steps to reproduce: 1. Connect with subdomain_admin to PVWA 2. Perform an Ad-Hoc connection 3. Create a clean machine for PSM installation 4. Run all installation automation steps including registration with tenant_admin user. Current Results: The registration step fails. Expected Results: The registration step should pass with warning: Failed to create Safe PSMUnmanagedSessionAccounts. Description: ITATS048E You are unauthorized to Update/Delete Safe PSMUnmanagedSessionAccounts. ERROR - PSMCE011E Vault environment creation was aborted. Description: PSMCE008E Failed to create PSM safes FATAL - RGSCOMP008E Registering components aborted.
known_issues.workaround: Contact support to remove the user from the safe.

KI#00024778: CyberArk Flows - In Shell Flows Tenant Host Value is Pointing to Standalone Flows Hostname
known_issues.description: When executing flows within a shell tenant, the 'Flows tenant host' value is expected to reflect the shell environment. However, this value incorrectly points to the hostname of a standalone flows tenant, leading to misalignment in flow configuration and potential execution failures. Steps to Reproduce: Open a shell tenant. Create a flow with a form that includes a text field mapped to the 'Flows tenant host' value. Run the flow. Observe that the 'Flows tenant host' value is incorrectly set to a standalone hostname. Expected Behavior: The 'Flows tenant host' value should correctly reflect the shell tenant hostname. All flow configuration values should be dynamically adjusted based on the tenant type. Current Behavior: The 'Flows tenant host' value is incorrectly pointing to a standalone flows hostname. This misconfiguration may cause issues with flow execution in environments relying on shell-specific configurations.
known_issues.workaround: Modify the hostname manually.

Current Behavior: When users attempt to add an app, they receive the message: "Unable to add app as this feature is disabled via policy. Contact your administrator." Expected Behavior: Users should be able to freely add recommended applications from the catalog without encountering policy-based restrictions.
known_issues.workaround: Enable the 'Allow users to add applications' policy setting as documented in the CyberArk docs.

HINT: Replication slot names may only contain lower case letters, numbers, and the underscore character. This is caused by dashes/hyphens in the replication name not being handled correctly in the Kubernetes Follower.
known_issues.workaround: Avoid using hyphens in replication sets for Kubernetes Followers.

known_issues.title: KI#00024236: K8s Inject Client Cert endpoint caches enabled authenticators
known_issues.description: The command class for InjectClientCert uses InstalledAuthenticators.enabled_authenticators_str as a dependency, which means after the first time it gets loaded the value will never change. This did not cause issues before because the only way to update the authenticator allow list required restarting the conjur service and therefore reloading the class. Now with the API method for enabling authenticators a restart is not required, so the class will keep using an old version of the enabled authenticators string.
known_issues.workaround: Customers can use the old method of enabling authenticators when using the inject_client_cert endpoint.

known_issues.title: KI#00024654: OPM installation on AIX does not register OPM service to be started after reboot.
known_issues.description: There is a broken step during installation responsible for creating symbolic links of OPM service to proper /etc/rc.d subdirectories indicating run level - when it should be launched. Error Messages: There is a message on screen during installation/upgrade: CreateDaemon: not found.
known_issues.workaround: Manually create symbolic links for opmsrv in the following subdirectories: /etc/rc.d/rc2.d, /etc/rc.d/rc3.d, /etc/rc.d/rc4.d by running: ln -sf /etc/rc.d/init.d/opmsrv /etc/rc.d/rc2.d/opmsrv; ln -sf /etc/rc.d/init.d/opmsrv /etc/rc.d/rc3.d/opmsrv; ln -sf /etc/rc.d/init.d/opmsrv /etc/rc.d/rc4.d/opmsrv

KI#00024558: CyberArk Identity - Intermittent Empty Proxy Table Query Result Causes Connector Inactivity
known_issues.description: In rare cases, the cloud platform issues a query to the proxies_ table using a simple SELECT * FROM proxies_ statement. Although data is present in the table, the query unexpectedly returns an empty result set. Once the query executes, the result—whether empty or populated—is cached. If the result is empty, the cloud assumes no online connector is available and refrains from sending any further requests (including login attempts) to the connector. This condition persists until the cache expires or the connector is manually re-registered or restarted by the customer.
known_issues.workaround: To restore connector functionality when this issue occurs: Option 1: Re-register the connector. Option 2: Restart the connector service to invalidate the cached result.

known_issues.title: KI#00022924: Appliance Follower configuration can fail with `--debug` enabled
known_issues.description: When the --debug flag is provided to evoke configure, this adds steps to the beginning and end of the Chef configuration phase to update service config files for verbose logging and to restart those services. This includes the API (possum), UI, and Postgres. We have observed that a failure can occur when turning off the debug logs at the end of Chef configuration which causes the UI service not to restart as expected: runsv ui: fatal: unable to lock supervise/lock: temporary failure. Once the services are restarted, Chef then waits for the Conjur node to become healthy by checking the /health endpoint. However, this fails because the UI never becomes healthy. The expected behavior is, if the service fails to restart for a temporary reason, then it is retried for a period of time until it succeeds or permanently fails. When observed, this occurred for 2 of 40 Followers configured.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00016494: Unable to Install PTA SSL Certificate when step 14 failed
known_issues.description: Issue Description Unable to Install PTA SSL Certificate when step 14 failed Steps to Reproduce 1. Run /opt/tomcat/utility/certificateSigningRequestGenerationUtil.sh to generate CSR for the new PTA server certificate. 2. When prompt Subject Alternative Names (Example: dns:<DNS_NAME>,ip:<IP_ADDRESS>): input the following - dns:somedns,ip:10.10.10.10,pta.com 3. output should be: Unexpected error. See /opt/tomcat/prepwiz/logs/prepwiz.log for more details. keytool error: java.lang.Exception: Illegal item pta.com in SAN=dns:somedns,ip:10.10.10.10,pta.com 4. Run /opt/tomcat/utility/sslCertificateInstallationUtil.sh to import the new SSL certificate based on the CSR in Step 1). and add a presigned cer file Current Results [Step 1/1 - SSLCertificateInstallationUtil] This step requires Vault Admin credentials using CyberArk authentication, and a restart of PTA services. Installing SSL Certificate Chain (Root, Intermediate(s), PTA Server certificates): Caution: You cannot upload certificates with signature algorithms lower than SHA-256 Specify PTA Server Certificate full path: /opt/pta.cer Unexpected error. See /opt/tomcat/prepwiz/logs/prepwiz.log for more details. Private key has not been set up. First run '/opt/tomcat/utility/certificateSigningRequestGenerationUtil.sh'. Expected Results PTA certicate installed successfully. Error Messages Unexpected error. See /opt/tomcat/prepwiz/logs/prepwiz.log for more details. Private key has not been set up. First run '/opt/tomcat/utility/certificateSigningRequestGenerationUtil.sh'. Logs N/A Other Symptoms The certificate import would fail due to following conditions: 1. Step 14 was executed midway and cancelled without completion. This would invalidate the existing temporary CSR. 2. Step 14 completed successfully but the server was rebooted or PTA tomcat services restarted before Step 15 is completed.
known_issues.workaround: 1. Run Step 14 to generate the certificate signing request 2. When this is completed successfully, back up the following files under /opt/tomcat/ca <pta_server>.csr <pta_server>.keystore <pta_server.keystore.password.tmp> <pta_server.keystore.tmp> 3. Submit the <pta_server>.csr to organization CA for self signed certificate generation. 4. When the self-signed certificate and CA certificate is ready, copy and overwrite all the 4 files in Step 2) into the /opt/tomcat/ca folder. Ensure permissions are unchanged. 5. Run Step 15 to import the self signed PTA certifcates and the CA certificate.

KI#00022729: PSMP command causes Vault to crash whilst processing syslog event
known_issues.description: A certain PSMP command (still unknown) can cause the Vault to crash whilst processing Syslog event 361 (PSM Keystrokes). The bug happens during the processing of the Syslog message code 361. This happens when a "command" is written to the audit from a PSMP session. This audit code needs to be part of the dbparm.ini parameter 'SyslogMessageCodeFilter', which by default for our internal monitoring it is present. Example command that causes the crash: !image-2024-07-24-10-59-56-377.png! Call stack from a memory dump taken during the crash: !image-2024-07-24-11-01-31-282.png! Haven't been able to reproduce the issue using PSMP yet. Tried multiple languages and control characters. Memory crash dump and supporting Vault logs uploaded here - .
known_issues.workaround: Remove audit action code 361 from the 'SyslogMessageCodeFilter' within the Vault's dbparm.ini configuration file. By default our internal CE syslog monitoring includes this audit code.

known_issues.title: KI#00023563: Identity CDS user end date cannot exceed December 31st 2050
known_issues.description: Issue description: Whether from the Admin console or through the API, one has the flexibility to set end dates until way beyond the Identity limit Identity will change all dates beyond 2050 December 31st to 19XX dates, which is to be considered as a bug For instance, January 1st 2051 will be changed by Identity to January 1st 1951... (both from console and API). The product should either accept all the dates the user is able to set, or should trigger an error if the user enters a date that goes beyond the technical limit. The current behavior can lead to very problematic discrepancies as some systems set end dates to 2099 (because they can't have users without end dates for instance). So, when provisioning these dates to Identity, they will be changed to 1999 and users won't be able to authenticate to Identity.

known_issues.title: KI#00024609: drfilessync.service status can be misleading
known_issues.description: systemctl status drfilessync.service can be misleading. This is especially problematic if you are using systemctl list-units --failed to monitor services to ensure they are all healthy. This is due to: Once the Primary PTA is turned off, the next time drfilessync.timer triggers the running of drfilessync.service, it will go into a failed state due to a timeout, as the Primary can't be reached any more. This is expected, however then upon Promotion to Primary, the failed service flag remains. Also, if you perform the failover fast enough, this will not appear as the failover happens before the next drfilessync.timer trigger.
known_issues.workaround: run systemctl reset-failed drfilessync.service to clear the failed state after promotion.

KI#00024594: Allow escape character in LDAP DistinguishedName and use relative base DN in LDAP search
known_issues.description: When the connector tries to extract a filter to search for a user in the LDAP server, the code only slices the portion before the first comma. This results in an invalid filter since the result ends with the escape character "\" if the next character happens to be a comma. For example, DN like "cn=France \, West,cn=group,ou=Department,dc=example,dc=com". Also, the search query uses a fixed base DN specified in the LDAP configuration, which is usually dc=example,dc=com. There could be multiple objects with the same CN segment but in different OUs. Example error messages from connector logs: Idaptive.Cloud.Core.Interfaces.LDAPException: Exception performing LDAP operation: Invalid RDN: unterminated attribute name. Idaptive.Cloud.Core.Interfaces.LDAPException: Exception performing LDAP operation: The search filter is invalid.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

As SIA is an internal API user, it should use the SIA internal service instead of the PVWA API. This would allow use of the strong account without additional permissions being required in PVWA.

known_issues.title: KI#00024425: SIA-RDP - Proxy IP sent in the RDP protocol rather than connector IP
known_issues.description: When performing an SIA-RDP connection, the proxy IP is sent as the source workstation in NTLM authentication and as the source IP in the SIA connection. The connector IP should be sent rather than the proxy IP, since the RDP connection to the target comes from the connector. This issue can cause customers who have CrowdStrike on their machines to block the RDP connection since it doesn't recognize these proxy IPs
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00024378: SIA-RDP - Domain ephemeral user creates additional user that isn't deleted
known_issues.description: SIA RDP using ZSP is hanging and not fully removing one ephemeral user. Domain ephemeral user account creation resulted in two domain accounts being generated. Failure pattern is this: > The ephemeral user service creates a user via LDAP. > We tried to reset the password but failed. > It's considered a retriable error, so we generate another user and retry allocation despite already generating a user. > A second user gets generated. > We never deprovision the first user, as provisioning never gets told about it.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00023898: SIA-RDP - Customer received invalid connection string on reconnect
known_issues.description: When a user attempts to reconnect, the flags-parser rejects the session cookie that is passed into the proxy as the new username, resulting in an invalid connection string login. This confuses the users, who believe their username is incorrect rather than this being tied to a reconnection/lost connection. The user should either be advised to close the session, or have the session closed for them if we see something that looks like a session cookie being passed to us.
known_issues.workaround: Restart the session.
