known_issues.description: Using the long/big dash character or other multi-byte characters in the PVWA configuration, for example in a Command in the platform settings, can cause PSMP to crash after giving the error: PSMPPS037E PSM SSH Proxy has been terminated. (Diagnostic information: XERCES error (Reason: invalid multi-byte sequence, Code:78), 78). The PVWA should check for unsupported characters when adding the command and / or PSMP should check for bad characters in the policies.xml file when loading it during runtime.
known_issues.workaround: Remove the multi byte characters and use standard single byte ASCII characters.

known_issues.title: KI#00016032: Pimsu ls cmd fails on directory with huge amount of files
known_issues.description: When running the ls command on a direcotry with a huge amount of files (huge = 20k), the opmd gets stuck and the pimsu returns "Timeout has expired" instead of running the command. to reproduce it: follow instructions on sf ticket: 02335014 or: copy the attached script to /tmp/<somefolder> on your configured OPM machine. give the script execute permission and run it. run the cmd: /opt/CARKopm/bin/pimsu ls /tmp/*
known_issues.workaround: Avoid the ls command on directories large enough to encounter the issue or reduce the number of files in the directory low enough to not cause the timeout.

known_issues.title: KI#00016046: Commands with empty string argument fails to be executed / denied
known_issues.description: When entering pimsu commands with empty string arguments, the command fail to execute. To replicate: /opt/CARKopm/bin/pimsu ls -l /tmp '' This is fails to be parsed by the OPM server.
known_issues.workaround: Avoid invoking commands with empty string arguments.

known_issues.title: KI#00016038: /tmp/OPMXXXXX lock files are not cleaned properly
known_issues.description: After some pimsu sessions, the /tmp/OPMXXXXX lock file of the session is not cleaned properly. This may cause an issue when pimsu tries recreate the file, but fails due to it already existing. The error received due to this is usually "permission denied".
known_issues.workaround: Manually delete the /tmp/OPMXXXXX files at issue. A cron job to delete old unused lock files can be setup to remove them automatically.

known_issues.title: KI#00017132: OPM cause log flooding on the vault after losing track of long term services
known_issues.description: OPM causes a flood of error messages on the vault when a process that has restricted shell on it executes a child process (another command) after the OPM that originally created the original restricted shell has restarted. Usually when working with a restricted shell, once you exit your own shell, all sub commands end as well (this is the default behavior of most shells.). Meaning, that the OPM is still alive as all the restricted shells exit. When the now not tracked process tries to execute a command, the request fails due to the process being untracked, and the query to the vault is sent with bad parameters, causing a large amount of log entries.
known_issues.workaround: On the OPM, track down the problematic restricted shell processes (you can check all the processes with restricted shell enabled which are older than the opm server). Change/add the acl to disable restricted shell for the processes found. Restart the process/service - with the new ACL configuration.

known_issues.title: KI#00018638: journalctl output not visible when using pimsu
known_issues.description: When using pimsu to execute the journalctl command, which can be enabled by adding the ACL '/usr/bin/journalctl.*', the command fails to produce any output. Example: $ pimsu journalctl $ When using journalctl without any options, it will try to pipe the output to a binary called 'pager'. When this binary isn't found, the command crashes and no output is written.
known_issues.workaround: Workaround 1: Use "journalctl --no-pager" instead of the "journalctl". This will show the output from command to the screen. The alternate command has to be done every time the command is ran. This will disable the pager functionality of journalctl. Workaround 2: Link the pager binary to the path '/usr/sbin/pager' Fore example: ln -s <full path to your pager> /usr/sbin/pager Common pagers can include "more", "less". In order to find a full path to a pager, you can use the command: "which <your pager>" This is a one time configuration on the machine and will allow the journalctl command to find a pager.

KI#00024427: Installation failure when setting PSMPSessionSeparationUIDRange in psmpparms
known_issues.description: During the installation process, it is not possible to define a valid range for the PSMPSessionSeparationUIDRange parameter in the psmpparms configuration file. Even when the parameter is set to syntactically correct and valid values, the installer fails and terminates with the following error: [ERROR] Parameter [PSMPSessionSeparationUIDRange] from [/var/tmp/psmpparms] is not set to a valid integer range. Using default value []. [ERROR] Invalid parameter values or failed to write into effective parmfile [/var/tmp/.psmpparms.effective]. Aborting installation.
known_issues.workaround: As a temporary workaround, the PSMPSessionSeparationUIDRange parameter should remain commented out during installation. After the installation completes successfully, the parameter can be manually configured in the PSMP configuration file (basic_psmpserver.conf).

known_issues.title: KI#00015794: Add a note for replacing NetPassword sdk in utils folder when upgrading
known_issues.description: Steps to reproduce: if you have cp install and you copied the netpasswordsdk.dll to the util folder in order to run NetAIMGetAppInfo, when upgrading the cp to version 12.0 the NetPasswordSDK.dll isn't get deleted from the utils folder. if you won't replace the dll to the new version the NetAIMGetAPPInfo will return error when executing it: AGINT006E A problem occurred while trying to complete hash calculation. Reason: Could not load file or assembly 'NetPasswordSDK, Version=12.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=40be1dbc8718670f' or one of its dependencies. The located assembly's manifest definition does not match the assembly reference. we should add to our docs a note to make sure the NetPasswordSDK is in the correct version here in the NetAIMGetAppInfo section: [https://docs.cyberark.com/Product-Doc/OnlineHelp/AAM-CP/Latest/en/Content/CP%20and%20ASCP/Generating-Application-Hash-Value.htm?tocpath=Administration%7CManage%20applications%7CApplication%20authentication%7CApplication%20authentication%20methods%7CAuthenticate%20with%20a%20hash%20value%7C_____1]

KI#00024497: CyberArk SWS – SAMLRequest getting lost when SWS auth is triggered in SP initiated flow
known_issues.description: When SWS authentication is triggered in a SP initiated flow for an application, it does not add the SAMLRequest state to the redirect URL and thus when the SWS callback comes to Identity it generates a SAML response ignoring the initial SAML request. Due to this, some SP's would fail as there are some SP's that strictly look for the "InResponseTo" property in the SP initiated flow before allowing the SAML to be authenticated. Steps to reproduce: 1. Create a SAML application in Identity. 2. Enable SWS for the application. 3. Login to the Identity User portal for an end user. 4. Launch the application in SP initiated flow. 5. Continue with SWS auth flow. 6. Check the SAML response generated by Identity for an end application. Current Results: SAML response token does not contain "InResponseTo" property. Expected Results: SAML response token should contain InResponseTo property matching with ID of SAML request.
known_issues.workaround: 1. Do not enable SWS until the issue has been fixed. 2. If the SP allows it, remove the requirement to check "InResponseTo" to accept the SAML with SWS enabled.

KI#00024492: PSMP does not update the PSMServer value in the configuration file during the upgrade
known_issues.description: Starting from version 14.2, PSMP supports real-time session monitoring. When upgrading from a version that does not support this feature (e.g., 12.6) to version 14.2, the PVWA displays the session as active, but the "Monitor" button does not appear. This occurs because the upgrade process does not update the PSMPServerId parameter in the basic_psmpserver.conf file on PSMP server, even though the Vault registration is completed. The value of the parameter in the configuration file begins with the prefix "PSMPServer_". Steps to reproduce: 1. Upgrade PSMP from v12.x to v14.2. 2. Reboot the PSMP server. 3. Create a PSMP connection. 4. Log in to the PVWA and move to monitoring page. 5. Confirm the Active session with the PSMP session. Expected Results: The Monitor button with the session appears on PVWA monitoring page. Current Results: The Monitor button with the session does not appear on PVWA monitoring page.
known_issues.workaround: Manual update of basic_psmpserver.conf file. 1. Log in to PVWA, and verify the current value of PSMPServerId parameter in PVWA by navigating to: Administration > Configuration Options > Options > Configurations > Privileged Session Management > Configured PSMP Servers. 2. Log in to PSMP server and open basic_psmpserver.conf under /etc/opt/CARKpsmp/conf directory. 3. Change the PSMPServerID parameter to the value where you verified it in STEP1, and save it. 4. Restart the PSMP service.

known_issues.description: When scanning a machine with Usage (s) that has a user set on it, they are found in Pending accounts as an account and its dependencies (Master user and its Usage). If the Master user set on the Usage (s) is changed, and set another user for that Usage, and then scanned again, there is no change in pending accounts at all. The issue is relevant to all Windows Usages - Scheduled Tasks, Windows Service, IIS, COM+. While the Usage should be updated to the correct Master Account, the Usage is not updated to the correct Master Account.
known_issues.workaround: 1. Using PVWA Accounts View (Classic), delete the Usage. 2. Wait for the object to be purge (after the retention is over). The default is 7 days, defined on the safe where the Account/Usage is located) 3. Run Windows Discovery to discover the Usage again.

known_issues.title: KI#00024312: Shared Ipad Login (Showing root detection screen)
known_issues.description: I'm encountering an issue when trying to log in to CyberArk on the company's shared iPad. The following error message appears: "Your device is rooted or jailbroken. You are unable to proceed." Could someone assist me? The customer is facing a root detection issue on a shared iPad. Investigation Details: The issue stems from how Apple handles file system access in Shared iPad environments. On Shared iPads, Apple allows access to specific system paths (e.g., /private/var/Users/...) as part of the expected functionality. However, our current root detection logic in the Identity Mobile application incorrectly interprets these legitimate paths as indicators of a jailbroken or rooted device. This behaviour is unique to Shared iPad and Shared iPhone configurations—especially when deployed through Apple DEP and managed via Intune—and falls outside the standard root validation scenarios our app is designed to handle. To address this, our team will enhance the root validation checks to better support this use case without compromising security. This will involve updating our detection logic to recognise Shared iPad-specific environments and safely allow expected path access without triggering false positives.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00024374: Incorrect Federation Selection due to the same IDP Signing Certificate across Multiple SAML Web Apps
known_issues.description: When a user initiates authentication via either the IDP Portal (IDP-Initiated Flow) and selects a specific SAML Web Application, or SP-Initiated Flow, the system does not consistently route the authentication to the correct federation. Instead, it matches the signing certificate in the SAML response against any federation using the same certificate. This can result in incorrect or inconsistent federation selection. Expected Behavior: Upon selecting an application in the IDP Portal, the user should be authenticated against the federation explicitly associated with that application, using its unique Application Key. Steps to Reproduce: Configure two distinct SAML Web Applications in the IDP Portal, both using the same IDP Signing Certificate. Associate each application with a different external IDP (in the same Identity tenant) and map each to a separate Mapped Directory Service. Initiate authentication by selecting one of the applications. Observe that authentication may be routed to the incorrect federation.
known_issues.workaround: Upload and assign a custom, unique certificate to each SAML Web Application in the IDP configuration to ensure correct federation routing.

known_issues.title: KI#00024344: [Docs] Fix outdated certificate rotation docs
known_issues.description: The `evoke replication rebase` step should be indicated as mandatory following certification rotation on standbys and followers, regardless of Conjur version. Otherwise replication can fail to resume on the nodes. Under Step 2: Import the new Leader certificate on each node of the cluster, update the NOTE under the evoke command example to read: "Upon rotating a Standby's private key, you must manually update the Postgres client key to re-establish data replication. To do this, run the following command in the Standby container to re-configure the data replication with the updated key." Under Step 3: Import the new Follower certificate, update the disclaimer to read: "Upon rotating the private key for the Follower, you must manually update the Postgres client key to re-establish data replication. To do this, run the following command in the Follower container to re-configure the data replication with the updated key."
known_issues.workaround: Run `evoke replication rebase <leader DNS>` following cert rotation on standbys/followers

known_issues.title: KI#00023068: When changing path for vault.ini as part of CreateEnv - the default path is taken instead of the new one (wrong vault IP is used)
known_issues.description: When running an interactive or silent CreateEnv in Linux (RHEL 8) and setting a different path for VaultFilePath, the createenv process ends successfully, but the vault IP that was entered in the vault.ini file at the new location is not the IP that was used. Instead the IP from the default location /etc/opt/CARKaim/vault/vault.ini is used. Steps to reproduce: Install CP, set an IP in default vault.ini, create new vault.ini at different path with different vault IP, run createenv utility selecting the different path for vault.ini. Current Results: Createenv uses the default path vault.ini IP instead of the one at the specified path. Expected Results: Createenv will use the path that was defined and the vault IP from that location.
known_issues.workaround: When having a different vault IP and using createenv, edit the vault.ini in the default path /etc/opt/CARKaim/vault instead of specifying a different path.

When the server is up and running, run /etc/init.d/aimprv status The output is: CyberArk Application Password Provider is stopped. *Solution:* vi /etc/systemd/system/aimprv.service adds the following lines in aimprv.service [Unit] After=network-online.target Wants=network-online.target
known_issues.workaround: Start the service manually: vi /etc/systemd/system/aimprv.service /etc/init.d/aimprv start

known_issues.title: KI#00020913: When accessing PVWA/RestAPI with an expired/logged off/invalidated token, currently valid tokens for that user a logged off/invalidated
known_issues.description: Scenario: 1) Account used to access RestAPI is (RestUser1) 2) RestUser1 is used to authenticate to RestAPI and receives token (<APIToken1>) 3) (<APIToken1>) is used to perform a search of accounts and accounts are returned as expected 4) RestUser1 is used to authenticate to RestAPI and receives token (<APIToken2>) (<APIToken1>) is invalidated since concurrentSession is not used 5) (<APIToken2>) is used to perform a search of accounts and accounts are returned as expected 6) (<APIToken1>) is used to perform a search of accounts and search fails due to the token being invalidated, as expected 7) (<APIToken2>) is used to perform a search of accounts , but search fails. The above behaviour is experienced not only with RestAPI, but also with PVWA authentication, which is built on top of the RestAPI. Expected behaviour is if an expired/logged off/invalid token is used, failure is received indicating so, but currently valid sessions for the user remain valid.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00024000: [Hardening] CPM+PVWA GPO for In Domain Servers sets the 'Remote Desktop Services' related policies to Never
known_issues.description: Hardening GPO policies for In Domain Servers sets the Remote Desktop Services related policies to Never. The hardening script itself sets the Set time limit for active but idle Remote Desktop Services sessions to 30 minutes on step RemoteDesktopServices.psm1, but only when it is a standalone installation (no PSM component). The GPO overwrites that value and sets also the policy Set time limit for disconnected sessions to NEVER. Expected Results: Time values should be limited, similar to PSM hardening policies: Set time limit for active but idle Remote Desktop Services sessions (Idle session limit: 2 days), Set time limit for disconnected sessions (End a disconnected session: 1 minute).
known_issues.workaround: Manually change the affected policies values.

known_issues.title: KI#00024270: CAVaultManager ReplaceLDAPDirectory: Fails with No Error Message if Query Fails
known_issues.description: If a user or group does not exist on the external directory, CAVaultManager will catch an exception and terminate but continue to print: CAVLT204I LDAP directory replacement completed successfully. Steps to Reproduce: 1. Run CAVaultManager ReplaceLDAPDirectory to see which users or groups would be moved. 2. Delete any user or group from the External Directory excluding the first in the list. 3. Run the same CAVaultManager command with or without the Update option. Current Results: CAVaultManager will stop before iterating through all users and groups. An inaccurate success message is printed. If Update option was specified, no changes are seen after starting the vault because an exception is thrown prior to committing the DB changes. Expected Results: A detailed error message should be shown when CAVaultManager terminates early.
known_issues.workaround: Run a support script to see the order CAVaultManager iterates through each group and user. Review the tables and delete users and groups as necessary until CAVaultManager can iterate through every user and group.

known_issues.title: KI#00023031: CP crash when Provider user and Application user are in different branch
known_issues.description: Environment: Credential Provider 14.0 and newer. CyberArk Application Password Provider service starts without issues but upon a request, via CCP or SDK, the service crashes. This happens when the installation installed the CP user in a different location. The installation succeeded, but when sending a GetPassword request, an error appeared. SDK errors: CASCU052E Failed to receive data (Rc=[10054]). CCP error: PDKTU004E Failed to receive data (Reason=[An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host]). APPTrace.log shows memory crash after PASVCFindUser.
known_issues.workaround: A) Move the Application users to the same branch used by the Provider user. By default, both are expected to be located in the Applications branch in the Vault user hierarchy. B) Set HideVaultUsersTree to No in dbparm.ini.

KI#00023677: Conjur Health API caches error responses
known_issues.description: The Conjur Health service has two expected responses: 200 OK when healthy and 502 Bad Gateway when unhealthy. The nginx API gateway caches the health response for 1 second to prevent gathering health information too frequently. However, if a request causes an unexpected error, this too is cached, and can cause the load balancer to inadvertently mark the node as unhealthy if it received the cached error response.
known_issues.workaround: Patch the nginx health service configuration to prevent caching error responses. Download 20_health.conf.patch, copy into Conjur container, apply with patch command, then reload nginx configuration with sv reload nginx. Apply on Leader, Standby, and Follower nodes.

KI#00023319: CyberArk Identity - MFA bypass does not use RelayState
KI#00024024: Running `evoke configuration apply` removes the synchronous replica settings
known_issues.description: When evoke replication sync start is run on the Conjur Leader, this updates the Postgresql configuration file with the additional setting synchronous_standby_names. However, this is modified outside of the Conjur configuration system as a whole. So when the Postgres configuration file is reconciled with the Conjur configuration, this setting gets inadvertently removed. The impact is all Standbys become asynchronous until the evoke replication sync start command is run on the Leader again.
known_issues.workaround: Run the command evoke replication sync start on the Leader. This must be done each time evoke configuration apply is run.

known_issues.title: KI#00023840: Fix interactive prompt handling for 'evoke role clear'
known_issues.description: When running the evoke role clear command without the docker -interactive flag an error results and appears to prompt without waiting for a response: error: undefined method chomp for nil:NilClass. This results because the input is not validated correctly where that prompt is implemented. This should be fixed so that the outcome is clear, when running this in a non-interactive shell without the --yes argument, and it should default to no action (the same as if you gave no response) and without an error message.
known_issues.workaround: If the operation can be run without user interface then appending the '--yes' flag to the command skips the problem.

known_issues.title: KI#00024022: Updating the Postgres configuration on a Standby resets its replica identifier
known_issues.description: Standbys are identified to the Leader with an application_name attribute of the database connection. The middle portion of the name is a unique identifier for the Standby. This application_name is used most significantly to configure which Standbys are synchronous replication candidates. However, when evoke commands are run that update the database configuration, these cause the unique portion of the application name to be regenerated. These commands include evoke replication rebase and evoke configuration apply. The consequence is that the Leader is no longer able to recognize that any synchronous replication standbys are available and database connections that modify the data will begin to block waiting for a synchronous replica to acknowledge the write.
known_issues.workaround: Reset synchronous replication on the Leader by running the command: evoke replication sync start

known_issues.title: KI#00023533: Conjur Standbys attempt to retrieve authenticator config from Conjur
known_issues.description: The Conjur API service does not run on Standbys, and so the authenticator configuration is not available on Standbys. However, the info/ service on Standbys still attempts to query this information leading to errors in the container log: connect() failed (111: Connection refused) while connecting to upstream. This error is relatively harmless and does not indicate a problem. However, this error log is preventable and causes confusion for customers when they see it in the Standby logs. Instead, the info service should detect when it is running on a Standby and return a message that authenticator configuration is not available on Standbys.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00024023: Replication slot cleanup processes can hang indefinitely waiting on the database
known_issues.description: There are two scripts run as cron jobs on regular intervals that interact with the Conjur Postgresql database. If there are conditions in the database that cause the database connections to wait (e.g. for synchronous replication acknowledgement), then these processes will also wait and not exit until the database connection returns. In scenarios where the database connection will never return, these processes and their corresponding connections can accumulate when the next cron job runs. This can lead to an eventual exhaustion of database connections.
known_issues.workaround: If the database connections are exhausted, restart the database. This will force the connections to close and the accumulated processes will exit.

known_issues.title: KI#00005578: Transaction failure recovery issue when using PSM gateway users
known_issues.description: When the Vault is up and running, and a user is logged on to the Vault through a gateway user, a call is made to "CCACSession::WriteAuditLog". As per this, often the Vault restarts, or the PSM component restarts, a recovery process should happen and the user should be re-logged on again. This also occurs if the components attempts another logon to the Vault after already being logged on. It doesn't necessarily have to be only during Vault shutdowns. However, when calling CCACSession::WriteAuditLog again, an error shows up in the Vault trace log: ITATS369E Service SafeDetails was not allowed for agent User <Username> The problem is that one of the recovery actions (CCASSessionTransactionsMgr::DoesSafeOpenNeedConfirm) calls the SafeDetails service, but this service does not allow gateway users to call it.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00021802: PSM: Incorrect Amount of Active Sessions Displayed in Dashboard
known_issues.description: The System Health page can show active PSM sessions even when there are no sessions active. The number does not change when PSM or PSMP servers are powered off. This can happen if a session is corrupted. (For example, if the PSM service is stopped while there are active sessions).
known_issues.workaround: For the self-hosted environment: Search the PSM Console logs for 'corrupted' and make a list of all the session IDs that are flagged as corrupted. Connect to the Vault with the PrivateArk client and open the PSMRecordings Safe. For each session ID, find the corresponding .session file and open Properties. Select the File Categories tab and find the PSMEndTime category and set a value as a Unix Epoch timestamp. For the Privilege Cloud environment, please reach the technical support team.

KI#00023912: Authentication Failure with Pre-Authentication Enabled after v13.X Upgrade
known_issues.description: After upgrading the Vault to v13.X or a version that has to pass through 13.X as part of its upgrade process, the logon transaction (with pre-authentication enabled) will fail with the error: ITAPE287E System error (Code: 287). Steps to reproduce: On a pre-13.X vault, enable pre-authentication secured session PAClient or PreAuthSecuredSession=Yes in vault.ini for a component. Ensure vault certificate has public key size of 4096 bits. Upgrade to v13.X. Start the component or attempt to logon via PAClient. Other Symptoms: The logon transaction START message will include FROM 0.0.0.0 instead of the actual client IP address. This has been seen to occur with OPM components as well.
known_issues.workaround: Either: (1) Run CACert Uninstall to replace current vault certificate with new self-signed certificate (2) Replace certificate with CA-signed certificate (must use 4096 bit key size)

KI#00021506: Evoke commands override `evoke variable`/conjur.conf settings
known_issues.description: The conjur.conf configuration file in the Conjur Enterprise appliance is used to set customer specific values for a few configuration parameters, as well as stores settings related to the auto-failover cluster. This file may be updated either by manually editing the file, /opt/conjur/etc/conjur.conf, in the appliance, or by using the evoke variable commands. Currently, the appliance has a defect where values set in this file by a customer are deleted when other evoke commands are run that modify this config file for auto-failover settings, such as evoke cluster enroll and evoke role promote.
known_issues.workaround: The workaround is to apply the configuration settings to service specific files, rather than to the global conjur.conf. Each service has an individual configuration file in the /opt/conjur/etc directory that is not overwritten by evoke. These config file settings are read by the services as environment variables, so another alternative is to set the parameters as environment variables on the appliance container with Docker or Podman.

KI#00023627: Chrome and Chrome Driver Processes not Terminated when Plugin Terminates
known_issues.description: the issue comes when CPM timeout is reached but plugin hasn't finished running and then CPM terminates the engine+plugin, but the resources used by plugin are not released, and in the next plugin run, the plugin fails because of it. Suggestion for the fix: We as the plugin, want to be able to receive from CPM (via engine) the signal to terminate the plugin run gracefully, so that the plugin will stop what its doing, close all the resources and only then terminate. This way the next run of the plugin will not fail because of resources still being used.
known_issues.workaround: Increase timeout for the plugin and close all the leftover resources

known_issues.title: KI#00023297: ITATP152E Error Using SHA384 - Vault only checks for SHA1, SHA256, and SHA512 as Signature Algorithm
known_issues.description: Vault only checks for SHA1, SHA256, and SHA512 as Signature Algorithm Before Printing ITATP152E. Steps to reproduce: CA provides certificate using SHA-384 as the signature algorithm. Follow Step 3 to Install certificate. Current Results: Self-signed Vault Certificate is replaced. However, we will see the following upon vault startup: "ITATP152E The Signature Hash Algorithm of the Vault certificate validation failed. Reason: Certificate algorithm could not be found" Expected Results: The error should not be printed. The vault should be able to detect SHA384 similar to other algorithms.
known_issues.workaround: The error can be ignored. The best workaround is to use a certificate chain with each signature algorithm set to SHA512.

KI#00016019: PSMSC042E Failed to create registry key [Software\Classes\CLSID] (System error [1021]: Cannot create a stable subkey under a volatile parent key.)
known_issues.description: PSM Session fails with these errors in the PSMTrace.log: PSMSC001E Session common exception occurred. PSMSC042E Failed to create registry key [Software\Classes\CLSID] (System error [1021]: Cannot create a stable subkey under a volatile parent key.) (Codes: -1, 1021) PSMSR009I Privileged Session Manager exception occurred. PSMSR519E [SessionID] Failed to map local drives for session [5] (Error: PSMSC042E Failed to create registry key [Software\Classes\CLSID] (System error [1021]: Cannot create a stable subkey under a volatile parent key.)) (Codes: -1, -1) PSMSR370I [SessionID] Attempting to release mutex for shadow user [PSM-ShadowUser] PSMSR523I Successfully released mutex for shadow user [SessionID]
known_issues.workaround: Follow knowledge article #000007065 Forcing the default profile to have the key created. - Loaded new hive from c:\users\default\ntuser.dat - Named loaded hive as TEST, under HKEY_USERS - Then created the required key on HKEY_USERS\TEST\SOFTWARE\CLSID and HKEY_USERS\TEST\SOFTWARE\CLASSES\CLSID - After that unloaded the TEST hive. - Delete the Shadow-User windows profile. - Allow the profile to be recreate by another new session.

KI#00023566: PSMPPS098E Failed to delete old log files from folder
known_issues.description: Following a compiler upgrade, the behavior of passing paths for cleaning outdated log files has been altered, resulting in incorrect handling of directory paths. This issue manifests in the following error being logged: PSMPPS098E Failed to delete old log files from folder. The path is not being passed correctly to the log-cleaning routine, leading to an empty value for the log directory path. This is an invalid and unacceptable configuration that prevents the proper execution of the log-cleaning process. It is important to note that this issue impacts the entire log retention functionality.
known_issues.workaround: The current workaround involves implementing a dedicated external process to manage the removal of outdated log files. This can be achieved, for instance, by configuring a scheduled task using a cron job.

known_issues.description: General description: * When attempting to restore only the recordings from a PSM Recordings safe, the safe is restored but the videos cannot be viewed in PVWA. * So far, we have tested major releases of v12.6 and v14.0. Steps to reproduce: * Create two standalone vaults (VaultA and VaultB), two PVWAs, and two PSMs. * Connect a PVWA and PSM to each standalone vault. * Perform a recorded, privileged session on the clients connected to VaultA. * Perform a full replication of VaultA and restore the recordings safe to VaultB. Current Results: * The restore process finishes successfully. However, the recordings cannot be viewed in PVWA. Expected Results: * According to the following document, we should be able to watch the restored recordings. Error Messages: n/a
known_issues.workaround: Restore the entire vault rather than only the one recordings safe.

KI#00021534: Dual Control is limit to 24 days
known_issues.description: Dual Control session terminates very close to its start, even though the dual control timeframe is still valid for many days, and PSM should not terminate the session. In order to reproduce - configure: 1. Set MaximumTimeframe greater than 36000 minutes (equals to 25 days). 2. Set EnforceDualControlTimeframeOnPSMConnections to Yes. 3. As an end user (non admin), create a dual control request with a timeframe for at least 25 days. 4. Approve the request as an Admin. 5. Connect with the weak user to a PSM session. On the PSMTrace.log: The error: "PSMSR1442E Dual Control time limit ended. Disconnecting session."
known_issues.workaround: Consider these suggestions as alternative workarounds: 1. Set EnforceDualControlTimeframeOnPSMConnections to No (that will prevent PSM from terminating dual control sessions at the end of the request timeframe). 2. Set MaximumTimeframe to a maximum of 34,560 minutes (equals to 24 days). 3. Do not request a dual control timeframe of more than 24 days (less preferable).

KI#00022337: Conjur health API hangs on local authentication check
known_issues.description: Under some circumstances, it is possible for the health check for the local authentication service to fail in a way that causes the health check to not return. The impact of this failure is that the failing call to the health API (/health) and all subsequent calls to it time out. A root cause for this failure is unknown, but is previously observed to occur in a Conjur Enterprise Appliance Follower running in Kubernetes. When this occurs, it may be remedied by restarting the local authentication service with the command inside the container: "sv restart conjur/authn-local".
known_issues.workaround: There is no known workaround to the issue directly in the Conjur Enterprise Appliance. In Kubernetes, an alternative to running the Appliance Follower is to instead use the Conjur Kubernetes Follower product: https://docs.cyberark.com/conjur-enterprise/13.1/en/Content/Integrations/k8s-ocp/k8s-k8sfollower-ovw.htm

known_issues.workaround: Reach out to the CyberArk support team for assistance.

known_issues.description: Plugin: Windows Desktop Local Accounts, Windows Server Local Steps to reproduce: # Install CPM and execute the hardening process for the server. # Create an account from the local CPM machine (you can use the new create account for the CPM service. # Manage the account (perform a change action). Actual result: # The account cannot be managed. Expected result: # Those account should be managed successfully
known_issues.workaround: Partial workarounds: (1) Local account can be rotated by using a Logon account, if the target account doesn't have Admin permissions. (2) Solution for handling 'Change' failures - https://community.cyberark.com/s/article/CPM-winRc-5-Access-is-denied-When-Trying-to-Manage-Windows-Server-2016-Local-Accounts (3) Solution for handling 'Reconcile' failure - https://community.cyberark.com/s/article/CPM-cannot-reconcile-its-own-local-accounts (4) - In case of trying to manage\rotate the CPM service user itself "PasswordManagerUser" (after the machine is hardened) - When applying the solutions above, the account will be rotated successfully, but the Usage itself (WinService) won't. (5) Use PMWindows.dll from CPM 9.9.5 if (2) or (3) does not work

This http redirect option needs to be turned off in the IIS settings both at the Root level and Default Web Site level, in order for the installation to succeed. Please select a different directory" Current Results: * Shows errors and the user cannot open the Z:\download directory Expected Results: * Users can browse into the Z:\download directory
known_issues.workaround: Saving file into Z:\Download directory: The user can select (highlight) the Download folder without stepping inside and click save. This will continue with normal behaviour and download file to local client downloads folder. Alternatively, customers can use Quest Toad with the supported out-of-the-box connector: https://community.cyberark.com/marketplace/s/#a3550000000EiC8AAK-a3950000000jjUiAAI

known_issues.title: KI#00015904: During SSH Key Reconcile or Change task, '^M' Character is added in the end of each key in authorized_keys file, which could fail corresponding apps
known_issues.description: When running a Reconcile or Change task for a Unix machine, while a key already exists in the authorized_keys file, the '^M' Character is added in the end of each key in authorized_keys file. When the corresponding apps reads the required keys, some of this apps may see the "carriage return" character as a part of the SSH key, which will cause a failure with their logon process.
known_issues.workaround: This issue is resolved in version 12.3+ of the SSH Keys plugin available in the Marketplace. https://community.cyberark.com/marketplace/s/#a3550000000EiCCAA0-a3950000000jjUmAAI

KI#00019039: CCP - certificate authentication failure
KI#00023283: PTA System Health Page - missing translation to different languages

KI#00015895: Reconcile fails with an error: "Execution error. EXT01::Sequence contains no elements. Error code:9999"
known_issues.description: During an attempt of account Reconcile which have a Logon account and the same Logon account was assigned the Reconcile account (or using the same username), while also target address contains an hostname with "-i" (e.g. "n11-it"), TPC will treat it as a key we will encounter the following error: "Execution error. EXT01::Sequence contains no elements. Error code:9999" In a full TPC debug log, we will also see the following errors: | Info -> bf :: c -> PRLoginExtraUser | Info -> bf :: c -> ***************************************************************** | ERROR -> b1 :: a -> General error occurred. Refer to log for more information | ERROR -> b1 :: a -> System.InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements at System.Linq.Enumerable.First[TSource](IEnumerable`1 source) at bg.a(IEnumerable`1 A_0) at bg.aa(String A_0) at e.a(bt A_0) at e.h(bt A_0, s A_1) at c.c(bt A_0) at c.a(bt A_0, df A_1) at c.a(s A_0, bt A_1) at c.c(bt A_0) at c.d() at b1.a(IList`1 A_0) at b1.c(String[] A_0) | Info -> b1 :: c -> RC: 9999
known_issues.workaround: One or more of the following steps will ensure to avoid such issue: 1. Do not define logon account and logon of reconcile account with the same username or the same account. 2. Do not define hostnames with "-i" inside. 3. Use the IP address instead of the hostname for the address.

known_issues.title: KI#00022624: CyberArk Identity - Generated password does not work in Bulk Upload
known_issues.description: Issue description: Customer want to add users using Bulk User Import, when he tries to give password which is generated while creating account manually from ADD USER page even it meets the policy requirements, the result is coming to his email with error as Password does not meet policy requirements. When he tries to do add user manually with same password he can do it. Here the problem is with Bulk User Import. The issue occurs when minimum and maximum password length are set to the same number.
known_issues.workaround: Go to Identity Admin Portal. Click on Core Services, go to Policies. Open default policy. Go to User Security Policies > Password Settings > Password Requirements. Change maximum or minimum password length but don't use maximum and minimum char length for same number, instead you can use different length. After doing above, please save and reload rights. After that try to upload sheet via bulk import.

known_issues.title: KI#00022061: iOS users stuck in authentication loop on CyberArk Mobile app
KI#00023173: HTTP Basic Auth functionality in WebApp breaks for Browser version 129
KI#00021563: Conjur seed service doesn't support concurrent seed requests
known_issues.description: When attempting to fetch Follower seeds concurrently, these requests may fail with a variety of possible errors, including: - "ERROR -- : Failed to produce seed file: Permission denied @ rb_sysopen - /opt/conjur/etc/ssl/..." - "FATAL: role root does not exist" - "ERROR -- : Failed to produce seed file: conflicting chdir during another chdir block" This is caused by the seed service attempting to produce seed files concurrently, which the seed generation process does not support concurrent execution.
known_issues.workaround: The workaround for this bug is to patch the seed service configuration to disable concurrent seed generation, producing seeds sequentially instead. Download the attached file "seed_run.patch" and place it on each of the Leader and Standby Conjur machines. Copy the patch file into the Conjur Appliance container, apply the patch on each node, and restart the seed service on the Leader. This patch applies to 13.0 and 13.1.

known_issues.title: KI#00018929: Syslog CPUUsage is consistently sending a value of "0"
known_issues.description: Within the system (SIEM, for example) receiving the Vault syslog CPU/Memory data, the CPUUsage displays as "0" consistently. When syslog debug is enabled (for example, SYSLOG(1,2,3,4)), the following can be observed within the Vault Trace logs: <syslog> <monitor_record> <Rfc5424>yes</Rfc5424> <Timestamp>Jan 01 09:00:00</Timestamp> <IsoTimestamp>2021-01-01T09:00:00Z</IsoTimestamp> <Hostname>hostName</Hostname> <Vendor>Cyber-Ark</Vendor> <Product>VaultMonitor</Product> <Version>11.5.0002</Version> <AverageExecutionTime>19</AverageExecutionTime> <MaxExecutionTime>2686</MaxExecutionTime> <AverageQueueTime>4</AverageQueueTime> <MaxQueueTime>107</MaxQueueTime> <NumberOfParallelTasks>2</NumberOfParallelTasks> <MaxParallelTasks>40</MaxParallelTasks> <TransactionCount>1736</TransactionCount> <CPUUsage>0</CPUUsage> <MemoryUsage>15</MemoryUsage> <DriveFreeSpaceInGB>100</DriveFreeSpaceInGB> <DriveTotalSpaceInGB>199</DriveTotalSpaceInGB> <SyslogQueueSize>220</SyslogQueueSize> </monitor_record> </syslog> In the code there is implantation for a
known_issues.workaround: when the API output is negative randomly, then we send back hard coded 0 as a workaround for this issue. Restarting the Vault server will fix the correct return value from the API.WorkaroundRestarting the Vault server will fix the correct return value from the API.

KI#00023010: Windows Scheduled Task password management fails when NTLM is disabled
known_issues.description: When NTLM is disabled using Local Security Policy: Network security: Restrict NTLM: Outgoing NTLM traffic to remote servers set to Deny All, Windows Scheduled Task password management fails. In Debug log, messaging abruptly ends after logging: Connecting to machine. In Error log error is recorded: Failed to connect to remote machine of task. Error: 0x80070721 Message: A security package specific error occurred. In Network capture it can be seen that Kerberos is initially being attempted and failing due to Service Principal Unknown (KRB5KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN). An incorrect SPN is being sent to the KDC in the request (krbtgt/host/ instead of host/<address>). Inability to look up SPN is causing Kerberos failure and downgrade to NTLM. With NTLM disabled, connection and management fail.
known_issues.workaround: No workaround has been found yet. Plugin is currently only functional with NTLM enabled

KI#00023033: CPM password handler should respect MinDelayBetweenRetries
known_issues.description: In case of timeout for the ChangeTask, CPM should correctly handle times between task retries. In the reproduction timeout scenarios, there is time inconsistency with handling steps between Starting Password Handler and Ending Password Handler. Steps to reproduce: Set MinDelayBetweenRetries to e.g. 5 min, setup account to which connection will result in a timeout (fake or powered off machine), trigger password change, wait for a few tries. Current Results: CPM postpones every second try due to 'was found but not changed since the minimum delay between retries had not elapsed.' The second attempt starts too early and does not respect MinDelayBetweenRetries value. Expected Results: The Password handler should respect the MinDelayBetweenRetries value between attempts/retries.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00022428: Replication slot cleanup job inadvertently deletes internal replication slots
known_issues.description: Conjur 13.1+ includes a utility to prune replication slots of inactive Followers as an hourly cron job. This utility contains a defect in that it deletes all inactive replication slots and not only those created for Followers (these begin with a "follower_" prefix). The impact of this defect is that it's possible for the cleanup utility to remove internal data synchronization replication slots if the cron job runs while an initial data sync is in progress to a Follower. This initial data sync occurs when a Follower is initially configured and when a Follower is rebased. When these internal replication slots are deleted, it can cause the data replication connection to that Follower to fail, requiring it to be manually rebased.
known_issues.workaround: The workaround for this bug is to patch the replication slot cleanup script to prevent it from inadvertently deleting temporary table sync replication slots. Download the attached file cleanup_replication_slots.sh.patch and place it on each of the Leader and Standby Conjur machines. Copy the patch file into the Conjur Appliance container, then apply the patch on each node. The cleanup tool runs as an hourly cron job, and the next iteration after patching will use the updated version.

known_issues.title: KI#00022429: Conjur Follower health API does not indicate replication failures
known_issues.description: The Conjur health API does include a section on logical replication status. However, these status details fail to capture or make visible all of details required to understand if the data replication is working as expected and if all of the expected data has replicated or not. For example: Has the bulk initial table data sync completed? Is it experiencing an error? There is a sync_error_count attribute exposed by Postgres. However, this is since the subscription was created, so it can't be used to know if there are current issues or not. When there are errors preventing either initial or streaming replication updates, this is not reflected in either the overall (ok) or degraded status to indicate this Follower may not be able to handle requests as expected.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00022621: Bad performance for 'OlacUserSafeFilesPermissionsListServ' transaction
known_issues.description: Bad performance for 'OlacUserSafeFilesPermissionsListServ' transaction, depending on size of database tables. This is affecting the PVWA Monitoring page, resulting in customers not being able to load this page at all. PVWA calls the transaction 'UserSafesListServ' which in turn calls the chain issue transaction 'OlacUserSafeFilesPermissionsListServ'. Depending on the specific table sizes this can cause this transaction to run for over a 1 minute. As the PVWA Monitoring page has to load a number of recordings depending on the filter, it has to run this transactions against each recording found. For example, 50 recording files found with the filter and the transaction takes 1 minute to run. In total it would take over 50 minutes for this page to load. This makes the page un-usable.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00022606: Restore/Upgrade fails with Selective Replication disabled
known_issues.description: When selective replication is disabled, evoke restore attempts to clean up configuration settings specific to selective replication in case it was enabled when the backup was created. In some circumstances, the contents of the backup file solo.json is missing values expected during the cleanup, resulting in an error output from evoke: error: undefined method empty? for nil:NilClass
known_issues.workaround: To workaround this defect, run the command evoke configuration apply after evoke unpack backup and before evoke restore. Applying the configuration causes the missing solo.json values to be created with the default values. This allows evoke restore to complete as expected.

KI#00022523: Upgrading from 13.0 to 13.3 fails with pglogical error
known_issues.description: When attempting to upgrade from Conjur Enterprise 13.0 to 13.3 (with any intermediate steps in between) fails with errors: From evoke restore: Failed to load policy: 405 Method Not Allowed. From the Leader log: ERROR: permission denied for schema pglogical. When attempting to load policy: Write operations are not allowed. This is due to latent pglogical resources in the 13.0 database backup causing a conflict with policy load in 13.3.
known_issues.workaround: Create a backup from 13.0. Upgrade to either 13.1 or 13.2. Run the command on the upgraded Leader: docker/podman exec -u postgres container-name psql -d conjur -c "DROP EXTENSION IF EXISTS pglogical CASCADE; DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS pglogical CASCADE;" Create a backup from the current Leader. Upgrade to 13.3 with the new backup.

known_issues.title: KI#00020355: Some hardware setups can causes Vault slowness
known_issues.description: Some hardware setups causes Vault cross system slowness after environment hardware upgraded/changed and enable more highly-concurrent threading. When the Vault server returns the query data back to client it requires to encrypt the data. When using multi-threading in a high load environment it can cause performance issues during the encryption before sending it to the client. This results in Vault slowness and transaction execution end time is delayed.
known_issues.workaround: As yet no workaround or fix is available. If this issue has been experienced after the Vault Hardware has been upgraded, we recommend to revert back to the previous hardware used.

KI#00022005: Vault Database service (MySQL service) out of memory crash with error "mysqld got exception 0xc0000005"
known_issues.description: General description: MySQL service may crash when out of memory with error: "mysqld got exception 0xc0000005" in VaultDB.log. MySQL crashes after SQL "SELECT" statement. One database transaction runs for hours, consume too much memory until the server reach "low virtual memory" and that crashes the database service (PrivateArk Database). Logs: mysqld got exception 0xc0000005 ; Most likely, you have hit a bug, but this error can also be caused by malfunctioning hardware. Thread pointer: 0x266df9b4c0
known_issues.workaround: Add to my.ini : [mysqld] internal_tmp_mem_storage_engine=memory

known_issues.description: General description: Flows UI does not accept value of 0 for the parameter "startIndex" for the function SubString when data is mapped Steps to reproduce: # Create a flow with transform node and create datastore # add a custom value # use substring formula in transform node and map custom value and enter startIndex value as 0, then we are able to save the flow but unable to run due to UI validation error. when you replace 0 with 1 then it works. # for mapped values the startIndex are taking values from 1. # for static values, startIndex is working fine from 0. Current Results: In substring formula, startIndex is not accepting 0 when data used is a mapped value. Expected Results: startIndex should accept 0 even for mapped data
known_issues.workaround: When mapping data in substring formula, use concat formula and add a character of length 1 in first field then map the actual data in the second field. In startIndex now we can use from values from 1 to length of actual data.

KI#00022427: Replication slot name collision in Conjur Appliance Followers 13.1+
known_issues.description: In Conjur 13.1+ there is a defect cause the database system identifier to be identical for all Appliance Conjur Followers. This system identifier is used to derive replication slot names for initial replication data synchronization when a Follower is configured or rebased. The consequence of this defect is that the replication slot names can collide when Followers are configured or rebased simultaneously. This collision means each Follower can interfere with the data sync of the others, causing replication to fail.
known_issues.workaround: The workaround for this issue is to run a command in a Conjur Enterprise Follower container after creating the container and prior to running evoke unpack seed and evoke configure follower. The outcome of this command is that Follower obtains a unique database system identifier to avoid data replication slot collisions. For Conjur 13.3: The command to run before unpacking the Follower seed is: docker/podman exec conjur-container-id evoke role clear --force --yes. For Conjur 13.1 and 13.2: run pg_dropcluster and pg_createcluster commands. After running the above command, the Follower seed may be unpacked and the Follower configured as documented. Finally, Rebase the followers with: evoke replication rebase conjur-leader-hostname

known_issues.title: KI#00013044: Error in log after configuring external storage
KI#00021189: File corruption occurred during CASOS 'PASVCFileWrite', Vault transaction 'WriteFileBlockServ', which can result in a PVWA outage
known_issues.description: The CASOS client on PSM and CPM component servers uses the save function 'PASVCFileWrite' to write to the "PVConfiguration.xml" file within the "PVWAConfig" safe. This translates to the Vault transaction 'WriteFileBlockServ'. This is used during an Install, Upgrade or Repair. One of these transactions failed due to going above the 30 second timeout value set, due to some type of network issue. This resulted in a corruption of the "PVConfiguration.xml" and/or "Policies.xml" file, which then affects the customer facing PVWA, which can ultimately cause an outage. When reviewing the relevant logs, the following error messages are observed: - CASOS debug log error: "Error (code = -1066012) is a comm error" - Vault Trace log: "ITAPE287E System error (Code: 287, Diagnostic information: XXXXX, 1, 0)."
known_issues.workaround: Potential workaround for now would be to increase the CASOS timeout value within the Vault.ini located on the PSM and/or CPM component servers.

known_issues.title: KI#00021179: Bulk imported users cannot be activated by admin once suspended after enddate
KI#00021493: Distributed Vaults: Bundle transactions are executed in Master when EVD points to Satellite
known_issues.description: We found out that some transactions (like UserLog) are executed in the Master Vault when EVD is pointing to the Satellite Vault. This is problematic as normally the Master Vault already has a lot of activity and this can affect performance in the environment. Issue observed when transactions are bundled, not observed when bundling is switched off. Comment from support: This seems to be a bug with bundle transactions since this was introduced to support the Distributed Vault flows. If the parameter DISTRIBUTEDVAULTS in the Vault.ini is set to static, yes or SRV (which are the available Distributed Vault values) then some transactions will run on the Master when EVD is pointing to a Satellite. Reports which use BundleTransaction could be affected: FileList, OwnersList, UsersList, GroupsList
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00022414: Extra whitespace in k8s service account token causes authentication to fail
known_issues.description: We have encountered customer support issues stemming from extra whitespace in the k8s service account token (extra newline) causing the k8s certificate based authenticator to fail. The presenting error in the logs is of the form: CONJ00048I Authentication Error: ExecCommandError: CONJ00072E Exec command failed in container with error WebSocketHandshakeError. Working with field engineers, we've identified the likely cause as configuration steps that add an extra newline to the stored Conjur variable. The k8s restful client used by Conjur seems to strip this and work as expected. However, the websocket client used for certificate injection into the k8s pod does not, and fails on the error above.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00021637: Identity Flows - Identity Flows Tile is shown even when the user does not have permission to access Flows portal
known_issues.description: General description: Flows Tile is shown even if the user does not have permission to access the Flows portal. We are showing the Flows tile if the login user has permission to the Flows web app, but the user should be also part of one of the Flows roles to access the Flows portal. So, if the user has permission to the Flows web app but is not part of any Flows role, we show the Flows tile and when the user clicks on it, Flows throws an error which is not expected. Steps to reproduce: Enable Flows entitlement and assign a user to the Flows web app but don't assign the user to any of the Flows roles. Now login as the above user. Current Results: The Flows tile is shown. Expected Results: The Flows tile should not be shown.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00016447: System Health Page Shows PTA as 'Not Installed' When Vault User is Not Located in Root Path
known_issues.description: Vault users that are not under the Root Location (/), although having the required permissions and are able to view the System Health page, see the PTA as "Not Installed", while users under root with the same configuration see PTA as installed. Can be reproduced by moving the same user from / to /System and back. While under Root PTA is showing as required in the System Heath, and while under /System, PTA is showing as Not Installed. Issue is resolved once the user is moved back to /.
known_issues.workaround: Move the user in question to the /Root location.

KI#00021398: Users can bypass Password/MFA through Windows Cloud Agent on RDP reconnect
known_issues.description: There are 2 scenarios for this issue: Lockscreen and Login. Steps to reproduce: Prerequisites: Windows cloud agent should be installed in target machines. Lock screen Scenario: 1. Specify a grace period in Identity portal (AdminPortal>Polices>Endpoint Policies>Common Settings>Agent Settings>Lock Screen>MFA grace period for OS X and Windows screen unlock>Specify grace period from drop down). 2.Login to windows machine through RDP client. 3. When session locks (must be locked with ctrl+alt+end or idle timeout screen lock), click the right arrow without entering a password until error. 4. Execute a Reconnect to the endpoint 5. Click right arrow to get password prompt. 6. When password prompt is displayed, don't enter password, right click arrow at password prompt 7. Successful RDP login Current results: Windows Cloud Agent Bypasses the authentication and user is able to unlock windows screen with empty password. Expected result:User should not be able to unlock with empty password. Login Screen Scenario: 1. Disable MFA, and apply only password in authentication policy. 2. Now connect to the WCA installed windows machine through RDP and login without entering password. 3. Successful RDP login Current Results: User is able to login without password. Expected Result: User shouldn't be allowed to login with empty password.
known_issues.workaround: Should enable endpoint authentication policy(AdminPortal>Policies>Authentication Polices>Endpoint Authentication>Select authentication profile having MFA. And remove grace period(MFA grace period for OS X and Windows screen unlock)

KI#00019732: Logic Container timeout for GetAllPlatformEffectivePoliciesCommand with a largenumer of platforms and concurrent calls
known_issues.description: The Logic Container GetAllPlatformEffectivePolicies command performance will degrade under certain conditions. With about 690 platforms, it seems that when it is run by a single request at a time the response takes around 2 seconds however when multiple users call the same command at the same time then all concurrent calls will take much longer, up to 2.5 minutes, The command itself would complete successfully however it had already reached the vault timeout threshold which would return an unsuccessful response. The root cause seems to be a combination of the number of platforms and command's internal processing time per platform. However the level of logging also affects the Logic Container performance, and a high log level can cause the timeout to occur.
known_issues.workaround: Reduce the LogLevel to 1 in LogicContainer\BLServiceApp.exe.config. Decrease the number of platforms and/or concurrent calls.

An AWS environment was used 2) Initiate a PSM Session (PSM-SSH) and write out several commands 3) Close session and play recordings 4) Play recording from PVWA or download from Vault PSMRecordings safe Current Results: 1) Recording can take a long time to upload to the Vault and become available in PVWA 2) The recording will display the first ~30 seconds as one and does not output each line typed individually. Expected Results: When playing the recording, this should be presented normally. Each frame should be presented as its own and not a chunk.

KI#00016681: Background scheduler service may crash due to OutOfMemory
known_issues.description: In a large customer environment crashes of a background scheduler service may encounter due to OutOfMemory issue. Reproduce: In large environment that has a lot of network traffic processed by the PTA, a crash to background scheduler service may encounter during the profile calculation of an "Anomalous access to multiple machines" algorithm. The crash may encounter due to OutOfMemory issue. The service recovers after the crash but the profiles for this algorithms are not created and the detection won't be available if there are no profiles available. Symptoms: AggregativeIce task consumes a large amount of memory and it is running for a very long time. Configuration details: * scheduler process has max 4G ( -Xmx4G) * AggregativeIce Task schedule: aggregative_ice_task_trigger=0 0 0 * * ?
known_issues.workaround: In order to avoid the crashes of the background scheduler service, disable the detection and the profile creation of "Anomalous access to multiple machines" algorithm by setting these parameters in systemparm.properties: aggregative_ice_task_trigger=0 0 0 * * ? 2099 disabled_detection_algorithms= AggregativeIceAnomalyAlgorithm

KI#00015040: Vault database deadlock caused by GetFile transaction
known_issues.description: The database deadlock relates to 2 transactions: GetFile and WriteAuditLog There may be a correlation between CPM high activity to deadlocks frequency occurrence. It is possible that other transactions will face deadlocks with GetFile transaction as well. In that case, investigation is needed (CyberArk Support will investigate). Symptoms: in the Vault trace logs: 1) ITADM111E Database Deadlock detected for user (null) (Deadlock found when trying to get lock; try restarting transaction) 2) ITATS651I Database deadlock occurred, Transaction will rollback. (Diagnostic information = 20,CPM_307) 20 = GetFile 3) ITATS651I Database deadlock occurred, Transaction will rollback. (Diagnostic information = 243,CPM_307) 243 = WriteAuditLog 4) If you add "innodb_print_all_deadlocks=1" to the Vault's my.ini (under PrivateArk\Server\Database), it will produce in VaultDB.log a deadlock between GetFile and WriteAuditLog transaction.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00021430: Follower API resource text-based search doesn't work with logical replication
known_issues.description: With the change to logical replication in 13.0 the text search index fails to update correctly on Followers. This causes search queries in the Follower API to return no results. Technical Details: After configuring a Leader and Follower and loading some data, the Leader shows data in the text search index while the same query on the Follower returns an empty search index.
known_issues.workaround: For Followers using "full" replication (not segregated replication), the workaround is to add the text search table to the full replication set with the command on the Conjur Leader: docker/podman exec -u conjur <conjur-container-name> psql -c "SELECT pglogical.replication_set_add_table('full', 'resources_textsearch', true, NULL, NULL)" This command does not need to be executed on Followers.

KI#00021194: Seed service gives misleading error when database connection fails.
known_issues.description: From the connected case, the customer reported a message from the Follower configurator logs: ``` curl: (22) The requested URL returned error: 400 [configure-follower] ERROR: Could not download seed file from master: Replication set 'full' not found ``` However, the Leader logs include a next-level cause of the error: ``` postgres 947960 - [meta sequenceId="121"] [7-1] FATAL: sorry, too many clients already evoke-seed - - [meta sequenceId="122"] E, [2023-06-08T18:11:06.303845 #191] ERROR -- : Replication set 'full' not found ``` This error originates in the seed file evoke action on the Leader, where any failure is presented as a "Replication set ... not found" message. Instead we should distinguish between a "failure to check" and a "failed check" outcome.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00020623: PTA - CRL Distribution Point based certificates require manual firewall configuration
known_issues.description: Customer certs include a CRL Distribution point based certificate (the server using the cert needs to reach the distribution point on port 80 to download the revocation list) Mongo needs to check the CRL via port 80 to allow mongo to start. We don't allow port 80 outbound by default, it is only applied once PTA sees a PVWA is configured (though this can allow CRLs to work in already-set-up servers) The certificate check fails and mongo will not start.
known_issues.workaround: Run the following before running minimal prepwiz: iptables -I OUTPUT 1 -p tcp -m tcp --dport 80 -m comment --comment "\'Allow outgoing 80TCP port\'" -j ACCEPT service iptables save

known_issues.title: KI#00020874: CISSUP - SSL/TLS server supports short block sizes (SWEET32 attack)
known_issues.description: The customer found a vulnerability "SSL/TLS server supports short block sizes (SWEET32 attack)", this attack exploits block cipher collisions, and can use 64-bit block ciphers to compromise HTTPS connections. Legacy block ciphers having a block size of 64 bits are vulnerable to a practical collision attack when used in CBC (cipher block chaining) mode. All versions of the SSL/TLS protocols that support cipher suites that use 3DES as the symmetric encryption cipher are affected. Cipher block chaining (CBC) is a mode of operation for a block cipher -- one in which a sequence of bits are encrypted as a single unit, or block, with a cipher key applied to the entire block.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00021481: Ticket ID is not recording with Dual Control and DisableDualControlForPSMConnections enabled
known_issues.description: General description: Ticket ID is not correctly recorded in audit data when using the default out of the box ticketing system "ServiceNow" with Dual Control and DisableDualControlForPSMConnections both enabled against the platform. Dual Control is configured for Show/Copy password only - Users can connect to targets without requesting. The ticket ID is not correctly recorded in the audit activity when a future request for Dual Control is confirmed, and the user connects to the target before this request has come into force or expired. Steps to reproduce: * Configure ServiceNow ticketing system to work with CyberArk using the out of box ServiceNow configuration * Enable Ticketing against a Platform * Enable DisableDualControlForPSMConnections against the platform * Enable Dual Control against the platform in the master policy * An Account establishes a PSM session with a TicketID. * Perform a dual control request for Copy/Show with a future date and multiple access enabled * Higher privileged user confirms dual control request * Lower privileged user performs another PSM session with ticket ID Current Results: After the further request for Show/Copy dual control is in place, the ticket ID is no longer recorded in the Audit data Expected Results: With each connection the Ticket ID is correctly recording regardless of the configuration.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00019520: Verify Change Reconcile immediate tasks appear stuck
known_issues.description: When submitting a Verify, Change, or Reconcile immediate task, the tasks do not get processed. This will happen for all accounts of like platform, while accounts of a different platform operate as expected. The CPM runs a Logic Container command each minute that selects the tasks which should be executed. It does this by querying only tasks which has a TimeToHandle < now (now is the unix timestamp of the Logic Container). This means that the command should never return a future value. In some situations we have found that the command returns a future value which results in this condition. To identify if you are being affected by this issue, the following PowerShell can be used. Simiply update the $path variable to point to the PMTrace log to analyse, then execute the script. If it returns output such as the following, this is an indication of the issue being experienced: logtime tid Incoming tasks time Start of operate line ------- --- ------------------- ---------------- ---- 10/04/2019 | 14:10:50.185591 3996 1554930605 1554923450 CACPM778I Immediate logic: Incoming tasks time: 1554930605, Last tasks time: 1554922979, Start of operate: 1554923450.
known_issues.workaround: Perform the following steps to reset the condition in which a future value is returned. 1) Restart the Logic Container service. 2) In cpm.ini, add this (manually, it is hidden in the PVWA ui) - RunSearchesOnStartup and restart he CPM service. Possible Values: None (Default - don't run searches, use the File Category values) Immediate (Run the immediate, ignore the File Category) Periodic (Same as Immediate, but for periodic) All (run everything - Immediate + Periodic)

known_issues.title: KI#00020907: Clocks mismatch on the PVWA Monitoring page
KI#00021355: Block policy for USB drivers doesn't block previously cached files
KI#00021183: Disabling "View Global Agent Configuration" permission removes the Audit option from the policy wizard
KI#00020792: ENE sending false negative alerts around component user outages
known_issues.description: Description This known issue can only occur for components which utilize the PVWA API, when the Vault parameter 'ComponentNotificationThreshold' is set to 60 of lower for the relevant UserType and the component user has "Send email notification if component is not connected" enabled. The issue is caused when there are several logins performed for the same component user, one through the Vault and to the other login using the PVWA API Vault as expected. The batch job will not check the most recent one for the user and will only check 1 login at a time, so the "LastVaultAccess" value isn't always the latest one. This will then result in the notification emails being sent if the mentioned conditions are met.
known_issues.workaround: Either of the followings: 1) Disable the CPM Scanner service if this feature isn't in use. 2) Ensure the CPM Scanner or PSM is working successfully and can connect into the PVWA API. It is still possible that this could trigger a notification, depends on how often the CPM Scanner is used. 3) Increase the timeout for the idle component notification to be above 60 minutes: 70 minutes or above. Note: Support assistance will be required to update the parameter 'ComponentNotificationThreshold' if this is received in a pCloud environment.

known_issues.title: KI#00021006: PSMP on connection attempt produces misleading error logs
known_issues.description: During connection attempt via PSM for SSH, when nss is queried from process of a user with insufficient access to this file, error log is written: nss_psmp: /var/opt/CARKpsmp/temp/syntaxparser-conf.json.1.2: Failed to open file nss_psmp: Caught an exception during syntax parser generation: /var/opt/CARKpsmp/temp/syntaxparser-conf.json.1.2: Failed to open file nss_psmp: Caught an exception during PSPSParser construction: /var/opt/CARKpsmp/temp/syntaxparser-conf.json.1.2: Failed to open file This can happen for any version of syntaxparser-conf file. In this context, appearance of those logs do not come from a connection process problem (PSMConnect is authenticated later on in the process), as the connection is established successfully and PSM for SSH works correctly.

known_issues.title: KI#00020452: The Dutch translation is incorrect.
KI#00020924: PCloud - System Health page showing internal IP rather than public IP
known_issues.description: General description: The PVWA's System Heath page will show an AWS Internal IP instead of the expected public IP. This can affect either the CPM or PSM server. This is due to both components using the PVWA's API. The System Health page uses the value of "szLastClientAddress" for the relevant user seen within the Vault database table "vault_users". Rather than the values seen within the User Block. Both the CPM and the PSM server use the PVWA's REST API service. As part of this process is a key rotation which will rotate the API key every day. The "szLastClientAddress" value in the database will get updated on either a "Logon" or a "FullImpersonatePAAuthServ" TX from the component user. Depending on the timing, it is possible to get the System Health page stuck showing the wrong IP address. A way to switch this IP is to trigger another "Logon" TX (Restart CPM/PSM service). Essentially it is a timing issue based off two transactions "Logon" or a "FullImpersonatePAAuthServ".
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00021017: New section in Synchronizer config file causing upgrade problem
known_issues.description: When performing Synchronizer upgrade according to the upgrade steps from documentation ( https://docs.cyberark.com/Product-Doc/OnlineHelp/AAM-DAP/Latest/en/Content/Conjur/cv_Upgrade.htm?tocpath=Integrations%7CCyberArk%20Vault%20Synchronizer%7C_____11 ) there's a problem with missing configuration section. In the 13.0 VaultConjurSynchronizer.exe.config file, there's a section which was not there in 12.9. If it's missing, the app won't start: <runtime> <assemblyBinding xmlns="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:asm.v1"> <dependentAssembly> <assemblyIdentity name="System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource" publicKeyToken="cc7b13ffcd2ddd51" culture="neutral" /> <bindingRedirect oldVersion="0.0.0.0-7.0.0.0" newVersion="7.0.0.0" /> </dependentAssembly> </assemblyBinding> ...... <assemblyBinding xmlns="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:asm.v1"> <dependentAssembly> <assemblyIdentity name="Microsoft.Extensions.Primitives" publicKeyToken="adb9793829ddae60" culture="neutral" /> <bindingRedirect oldVersion="0.0.0.0-7.0.0.0" newVersion="7.0.0.0" /> </dependentAssembly> </assemblyBinding> </runtime> This has to be fixed either on documentation side (add this to the upgrade procedure) or in the app (remove requirement for this section).
known_issues.workaround: Add missing section to the config file

known_issues.description: During a PSM for SSH session, end users may get an error message after inserting a command with unusual characters, and the session will become stuck as a result. The following message is seen in the PSMP logs as well as the ITALOG on the vault server: ITATS921E Internal error occurred when writing log record (diagnostic information = 361, 4). The database fails this transaction because the size of the audit is more than the maximum size supported. This happens because the unusual character 0xA0 in ascii is changed in the vault to UTF8 and as a result is converted to 0xC2 0xA0, so the data chunk we send is bigger than what we calculated.
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

KI#00017348: Failed to Delete Safe Files because of file list length exceeds maximum length
known_issues.description: When deleting pending accounts with long file names using the REST API, the deletion fails. The reason for failure is that there are no limitations in the PVWA on the length of the account name. When the API call is ran, account deletions are ran in bundles of a maximum of up to 100 accounts per bundle The total character length of the bundle list should not exceed 22,000 characters. When there are long file names in Pending Accounts, this limit can be exceeded causing the communication to fail with the vault and ends the terminates the process. The following error can be observed in the PVWAApp.log: ITACM063S Communication error (Diagnostic information: 563,21996). (Vault [CAMainVault] safe [PasswordManager_Pending] Note the larger Diagnostic Information number after the comma can vary between communication failure errors and will not always match the example above. The communication error is followed by: Code: 500. Content: {"ErrorCode":"DAFWS277E","ErrorMessage":"Not all Discovered accounts were deleted. Please try again or contact your Administrator if the problem continues."}
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found
