known_issues.description: General description: The sharing of personal apps or secured items does not function properly with Google AD groups. Based on the current code implementation, we are only considering certain AD providers when displaying shared secured items. For example, although Google AD provider is used for sharing, it is not considered when displaying apps to the shared user. The current implementation should be updated to consider all effective user groups. To reproduce the issue, follow these steps: Set up Google AD for the tenant. Go to the User portal and add a new app and secured item. Add a Google AD group for sharing. Log in with a user within the shared AD group. The shared item is not visible." Current Results: At times, the shared item appears on the screen for a short duration (due to Signal R where a single item is retrieved and rendered on the screen). However, after some time, it disappears and never reappears. Expected Results: Sharing with Google AD groups should work properly

KI#00017274: Chrome connections fail after updating PSM with Chrome version 92 or later
known_issues.description: Sometimes when Chrome on the PSM server is updated to version 92 or later, and the PSM is hardened, the Chrome based connection components stop working. The problem is caused by AppLocker preventing Chrome from launching because it does not recognize the publisher of the new Chrome version. On some servers after upgrading to Chrome v92 or later the creation of an AppLocker rule for Chrome with type publisher fails with the error: "the publisher information cannot be extracted from the specified file: C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe Reason: The object identifier does not represent a valid object. (Exception from HRESULT: 0x800710D8)" When running the PSMConfigureAppLocker.ps1 script on the PSM Server the script fails to create the exception for Chrome and PSMAppLockerLog.log has this error message: PSMAppLocker.psm1 - ERR - Something trapped: The rules cannot be created. Required file information is missing from the following file: %PROGRAMFILES%\GOOGLE\CHROME\APPLICATION\CHROME.EXE
known_issues.workaround: The root cause is an issue in Chrome, but until this is fixed, the workaround is to use an AppLocker "hash" type rule for Chrome. In the PSMConfigureApplocker.xml file change the GoogleChrome Method from Publisher to Hash and rerun the PSMConfigureAppLocker script Note that the next time that Chrome will be updated the PSMConfigureAppLocker script will need to be rerun for the Chrome connections to work.

KI#00020826: Expired access token causes error in Conjur UI session.
known_issues.description: The Conjur UI uses a Conjur API access token stored in the UI session. A regression in 12.9 means it is possible for this access token to expire before the UI session itself expires. When this occurs, the UI is unable to access the data from Conjur and fails to refresh the access token or redirect the user to login again.
known_issues.workaround: When this occurs, the workaround is to clear the cookies in the current browser session or to start a new browser session with the Conjur UI. This will prompt for the user to login again and authenticate to Conjur with a new access token.

known_issues.description: PSM for SSH does not support forwarding custom sftp connection commands from custom ssh client to target sshd. This is due to hardcoded remote command in the ssh client when sftp makes connection with psmpserver. Psmpserver already has parametrized SFTPCommandRegex (configurable with PVWA), but the forwarding is omitted on sftp connections.
known_issues.workaround: To resolve this, the ssh client should forward matched subsystem aliases / sftp commands when performing an sftp type connection to target.

KI#00013966: PMTerminal - Password exposed in logs if the line is too long
known_issues.description: In PMTerminal, when we set Debug=Yes, we get the logs of the plugin execution. For each prompt line that is above 80 characters, the line will be splitted into 2 different lines. In case that password value was split by this behavior (if the character in index 80 is part of the password value), the password will be exposed in clear text in the log. For example, in iDRAC Plugin, the change command that we see in the logs is: /USERNAME-> racadm config -g cfgUserAdmin -o cfgUserAdminPassword -i 2 HALF_OF_PASSWORD_HERE OTHER_HALF_HERE
known_issues.workaround: 1. Use TPC instead of PMTerminal. 2. Another solution, which is less secure, is to work with a shorter password that will not trigger the 80 characters bug limitation, that will split and expose the password. 3. Our recommendation is not working with Debug=yes, which will not create the logs with the exposed password.

known_issues.description: Syslogs that are supposed to be matched with the plugin but its attributes are in different order. Current Results: Syslog doesn't match by the plugin Expected Results: Syslog does match by the plugin Error Messages: "No audit creator plugin was found for syslog:" followed by the syslog. Syslog example: <13>1548733866960 server 07/01/2018 11:14:56 AMLogName=SecurityComputerName=server.domain.comTaskCategory=LogonSourceName=Microsoft Windows security auditingType=InformationEventType=0EventCode=4732Message=An account was successfully logged on.Keywords=Audit Success ..... will work, but this <13>1548733866960 server 07/01/2018 11:14:56 AMLogName=SecurityComputerName=server.domain.comKeywords=Audit SuccessTaskCategory=LogonSourceName=Microsoft Windows security auditingType=InformationEventType=0EventCode=4732Message=An account was successfully logged on...... won't work, as the order is different
known_issues.workaround: Simply edit the matcher's regex to accept the syslog's format Please go through the following steps: 1. On /opt/tomcat/plugins/splunkWindowsAddUserToGroupPlugin.js file Replace var regex = new RegExp("<13>[\\S\\s]+SourceName=Microsoft Windows security auditing\\.[\\S\\s]*EventCode=4732[\\S\\s]*Keywords=Audit Success[\\S\\s]"); with var regex = new RegExp("<13>[\\S\\s]*EventCode=4732[\\S\\s]+SourceName=Microsoft Windows security auditing\\.[\\S\\s]*Keywords=Audit Success[\\S\\s]"); 2. On /opt/tomcat/plugins/splunkWindowsCreateUserPlugin.js Replace var regex = new RegExp("<13>[\\S\\s]+SourceName=Microsoft Windows security auditing\\.[\\S\\s]*EventCode=4720[\\S\\s]*Keywords=Audit Success[\\S\\s]"); with var regex = new RegExp("<13>[\\S\\s]*EventCode=4720[\\S\\s]+SourceName=Microsoft Windows security auditing\\.[\\S\\s]*Keywords=Audit Success[\\S\\s]");

KI#00020453: Reports screen is displaying placeholder instead of actual values
known_issues.description: The reports screen is displaying placeholders instead of actual values. Steps to reproduce: 1. Login to Tenant > Admin Portal > Roles 2. Add/Edit/Delete Members, create a new Role or edit any existing role 3. Navigate to Reports screen 4. Create a new Report or pick any existing reports and Query Events table 5. Sample Syntax for report Query: SELECT InternalSessionId, InternalTrackingId, WhenOccurred, FromIPAddress, EventMessage, EventType, NormalizedUser, Role, EditType FROM Event WHERE Event.WhenOccurred > @StartDate 6. Once the report is saved, we can reproduce the issue by Preview/Export. Current Results: Report is displaying place holders {Role} and {Principal} instead of actual values. Expected Results: Placeholders should be replaced with the actual values
known_issues.workaround: Workaround has not been found yet

known_issues.title: KI#00015892: CPM - FromHour, ToHour checks every 10 seconds in the window if configured to start on one day and end the next day
known_issues.description: The FromHour and ToHour parameters define the time frame in hours during which the CPM can change passwords, either manually or automatically. This is useful, for instance, if the organization only wishes to change passwords during the night. This parameter is not relevant if the platform will be applied to a member of an account group. Acceptable Values 0-23 or -1 for none. When setting FromHour on one day and ToHour causing it to roll to the next day we see that the service checks for changes every 10 seconds and we do not get the typical "your next check will be on <date and time>". Example: FromHour "16" and ToHour "3" will cause the problem. Steps to reproduce: -Define on a platform "PerformPeriodicChange" to "Yes". -Define "FromHour" to "16". -Define "ToHour" to "3". -Create an account and assign to the above platform. -Restart the CPM service (the current time must be in the hours range).
known_issues.workaround: Define the parameters "FromHour" and "ToHour" or before midnight or after midnight. Do not define values from two different days.

known_issues.description: Vault encrypts the password, which is then added in dbparam.ini. The Decryption of a password with these special characters doesn't produce the actual password and the HSM integration fails with error - CKR_PIN_INCORRECT
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround was found

known_issues.title: KI#00020308: Difference in the immutable ID on CyberArk and on MS side
known_issues.description: Users are not able to access O365 from Identity, getting error "User account from Identity Provider does not exist in tenant ". When checked, the Immutable ID of user in CyberArk doesn't match with the ID on MS side. In some places in the code when we compare the string (Immutable ID) we ignore the case however we keep the cultural aspects. So when the customer has a different language set other than English ("en-US") it returns the incorrect result. Expected Result: ObjectGuid should be displayed in GUID format Actual Result: ObjectGuid is displayed in Binary format (String starts with "[]="
known_issues.workaround: Ensure the following registry in connector machine have the following value. [HKEY_USERS\.DEFAULT\Control Panel\International] "Locale"="00000409" "LocaleName"="en-US" After setting the value above, restart the connector service and then perform user reload for the affected users in Admin Portal.

KI#00020525: For some clients based out on few regions, SAML based external IDP authentication is failing with Azure AD.
known_issues.description: Some users are having issues signing in with SAML, getting error : "Message: AADSTS7500510: XML attribute 'IssueInstant' in the SAML message must be a dateTime" This is happening because Issue Instant is being generated in format with dot as time separator in Danish language. Standard format uses colons, hence request is being rejected. Current value format : IssueInstant ="2023-02-24T09.28.27.000Z" Expected value format: IssueInstant ="2023-02-24T09:38:04.000Z"
known_issues.workaround: No Workaround has been found yet. Sometimes changing client's locale and language settings fix the issue sometimes it doesn't.

known_issues.title: KI#00020409: /UserMgmt/RemoveUser API returns error even though action succeeded
known_issues.description: If a service account calling the /UserMgmt/RemoveUser API to delete a user doesn't have an Email/Mobile but a MFA policy applies to the service account that requires Email/SMS MFA, the API call returned failure and exception is thrown indicating the service account may not be able to log in: Response: { "success": false, "Result": null, "Message": "The change you've made has resulted in a configuration that will block your login. Please make sure you have appropriate authentication mechanisms available to your account based on your changes. For example, make sure that your user settings include phone numbers and email address for use with phone- and email-based challenges.", "MessageID": "The change you've made has resulted in a configuration that will block your login. Please make sure you have appropriate authentication mechanisms available to your account based on your changes. For example, make sure that your user settings include phone numbers and email address for use with phone- and email-based challenges.", "Exception": "CyberArkIdentity.Cloud.Core.Policy.AdminLockoutException: The change you've made has resulted in a configuration that will block your login." } But in fact the user was deleted successfully, making the API response confusing.
known_issues.workaround: Make sure MFA policy is disabled for the service account or add Email/Mobile to the service account according to MFA policy.

known_issues.title: KI#00020478: While running PTA upgrade the 'Handling Network Interfaces' step fails in case of a missing network interface file
known_issues.description: When running an upgrade, there is a network interface that doesn't have a matching file in /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ As a result, the step 'Handling Network Interfaces' fails with the error: updating /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-ens192 sed: can't read /etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-ens192: No such file or directory Task handleNetworkInterfaces ended with code 7
known_issues.workaround: The suggested workaround in order to complete the upgrade process, is to shutdown the second NIC. It is possible to shutdown the network interface by running the command: "ifconfig ens192 down" Another possible solution is to create the missing file with default values. To do so, the file "/etc/sysconfig/network-scripts/ifcfg-ens192" needs to be created with default values. After either solution, run the upgrade again to complete the upgrade process.

known_issues.title: KI#00020467: PTA Agent crashes without specific configuration
known_issues.description: For environments where there are large amounts of fragments and packets, the agent keeps crashing if the following configuration is not set in the agent configuration file. Max_Fragments_Number=4 Packet_Filter = "not tcp port 445 and less 2000"
known_issues.workaround: 1. Navigate to C:\Program Files\cyberark\PTA Agent\ directory and edit the config.ini file. 2. Locate the [Debug] section header. Add the [Debug] section header if it is missing. 3. Add the following parameters to the [Debug] section: Max_Fragments_Number=4 Packet_Filter = "not tcp port 445 and less 2000" 4. Save the file. 5. Restart the PTA Agent service to apply the changes.

known_issues.description: CAVaultManager ChangeAWSKeys command fails if the ChangeServerKeys process runs longer than 15 minutes. During ChangeAWSKeys, There 15 min hardcoded timeout value (BaseSite::ExecuteChangeServerKeysCommand). We are waiting for the CSK to finish. If it exceed 15 minutes, we will receive timeout (the CSK process still continued) and the CAVaultManager operation failed. Symptoms: In CAVaultManager log: ITATP158E Change Server Keys command finished with errors. Reason: Change server keys failed. Code 1048575. Change server keys failed. Code 1048575 Note: ChangeServerKeys log will show it ended successfully
known_issues.workaround: A workaround or fix has not yet been found.

KI#00020254: Safes - Get all safes performance issue
known_issues.description: Using the 'Get Safe' API request in a large environment, 2000 or more Safes, results in a long response time. This occurs with both the v1 and v2 API. The response time is dependent on the total number of Safes returned and will increase as either the total Safes increased (v1 api) or the limit to return is increased (v2)
known_issues.workaround: Use the v2 API with a low enough limit that the response time is acceptable. The default limit value of 25 will be used if limit is not specified in the request. No workaround found yet for the v1 API.

known_issues.title: KI#00020167: Restored safe that contain dependency objects via PARestore utility to a new target safe do not restored all safe dependency object information.
known_issues.description: Restored safe that contain dependency objects via PARestore utility to a new target safe do not restored all safe dependency object information. When restoring a single safe, Dependency of an account is not restored with a link to the original account When Restoring a singly safe via PARestore utility, a restored safe that contains a dependency objects does not restore all safe dependency object information there for in the PVWA you are receiving empty line. This applies to the following dependencies in the V9 UI: Windows Server Scheduled Task IIS Application Windows Registry COM+ ApplicationIIS Annonymous User
known_issues.workaround: 1. Open the safe on the PrivateArk client 2. Rename the dependency object file. 3. From the PVWA add the dependency object 4. After verification re-login to the PrivateArk client and delete the renamed file from step#2 , in order to remove the empty line from the PVWA.

KI#00019007: CPM - Incorrect information being logged in the Hardening Script log
known_issues.description: The incorrect path is logged in the Hardening Script log, when the hardening process fails to add the PluginManagerUser to the PasswordManagerUser Windows OS profile. During the hardening process the hardening is issuing the following: (CPM_Password_Manager_Services_LocalUser.psm1) line 202: SetAclPermissions "PluginManagerUser" ($env:USERPROFILE + "\..\" + "PasswordManagerUser") "FullControl" "Allow" It is attempting to set "FullControl" permissions for PluginManagerUser on the path: $env:USERPROFILE + "\..\" + "PasswordManagerUser". If the currently logged on user is administrator, this resolves out to (c:\users\administrator\..\PasswordManagerUser) If this command fails; however, the entry written to the log file says: 2022-05-13 12:18:59 ERROR: Failed to grant access permissions on folder 'C:\Program Files (x86)\CyberArk\Password Manager'. It is logging the incorrect path where it failed to grant the access permissions. The reason it is logging the incorrect path is because the incorrect path is hard coded into the message in the hardening script. line 170 Add-CALogAll "Did not grant access permissions on folder '$global:cpmPath'. Path not found." "Error" $global:cpmPath resolves out to (C:\Program Files (x86)\CyberArk\Password Manager), and the log messaging is blindly using this path rather than the path that the SetAclPermissions command actually attempted to process against.
known_issues.workaround: Add the permission for PluginManagerUser in the PasswordManagerUser profile

KI#00019147: PSMP CyberArk signs JIT Certificates using the algorithm "ssh-rsa"
known_issues.description: OpenSSH updated the default allowed CA Signing Algorithms allowed: removing old "ssh-rsa" (RSA/SHA1) algorithm from usage of the Server sshd, Client ssh, and ssh-keygen utility. The short lived Just In Time (JIT) Certificates that are being generated by CyberArk are using this OpenSSH CA Signature algorithm. On newer versions of the sshd, this algorithm is disabled for usage by default.
known_issues.workaround: Update the Target Server /etc/ssh/sshd_config to accept the CA Signature used by CyberArk by updating/adding: CASignatureAlgorithms +ssh-rsa

KI#00018507: Negative TaskStartTime value causes looping reports
known_issues.description: In case the FileCategory TaskStartTime has a negative value this will cause the report to be run in a loop. For example: a report scheduled on Mondays with a negative TaskStartTime representing -4 hours will run on Sunday September 16th at 20:00 and when it tries to calculate the next run time it will again select Monday September 17 and adjust by -4 hours to get Sunday September 16th at 20:00 and will again run the report. This will continue until the report runs on Monday September 17th at 00:00 now when the report looks for the next available run date it will select Monday September 24 and adjust for -4 hours to Sunday September 23 at which time the report will again loop.
known_issues.workaround: No workaround yet

KI#00017956: PTA password rotations can lock out the PTAUser/PTAAppuser accounts
known_issues.description: PTA password rotations can result in the PTA accounts being suspended in some circumstances. Steps to reproduce: Have a busy environment/slow PTA or Vault Current Results: When PTA rotates the password while it has a vault transaction 'queue' it adds the password change to the 'queue' then it will add more transactions after the password change, however these new transactions will not have the new password that is about to be rotated. So the PTA rotates the PTAUser/PTAAppUser passwords, then the queued transactions continue to use the old password for a few attempts, resulting in a lock out of the account. Expected Results: The PTA should always use the correct, accurate password. Error Messages: Successful password change: LOG | [2021-11-28|03:00:45.179|EST] | DEBUG | \{aid=backgroundScheduler} | \{sid=} | \{rid=} | \{tid=tasksScheduler_Worker-1} | com.cyberark.diamond.background.schedulers.ChangePTAVaultPasswordTask.changeVaultUserPassword(ChangePTAVaultPasswordTask.java:47) - Password successfully changed for Vault user 'PTAUser'. Then a failure: com.cyberark.diamond.utils.ProcessResultReader.run(ProcessResultReader.java:51) - PTAUser logon failed
known_issues.workaround: Increase the number of attempts before the PTAUser and PTAAppUser are suspended.

known_issues.title: KI#00018345: Incorrect prompt message from PVWA when changing a user password and not meeting the security policy
known_issues.description: PVWA displays a security warning when a user changing their password at the logon screen enters a password that does not meet security requirements of the vault. This warning should match the settings defined in passparm.ini on the vault, but in the case of some PVWA versions the message displays the incorrect password requirements. To reproduce: 1. Create "passparm.ini" file with the content. [MAIN] CheckUserName=Yes NameTolerance=4 MinAlphabetic=1 MinNumeric=1 MinPunctuation=1 MinLength=10 MustMixCase=Yes MinUnique=4 PunctuationCharacters=`~!@#$%^&*()-_=+[\{]}\|;:'",<.>/? DaysForPasswordExpiration=90 PasswordsRemembered=8 MinAge=1 MaxRepeatingCharacters=3 2. Store the file at the "System" safe 3. Create a new epv user 'user1' with password "123Cyber" and check the "User must change password at the next logon" checkbox 4. Try to connect to the PVWA with 'user1' 4. When prompted to change password, set the new password to '1111111111" 5. Error message as a result of the security password violation is shown. Expected prompt: Error content message = "ITAPW003E Password must contain at least 1 alphabetic character(s)" Actual prompt: Error content message = "Your password format is invalid. The password must have at least 8 characters and no more than 16 characters. It must contains at least 1 number, 1 upper case letter and 1 lower case letter. No special characters are accepted" Comments: Currently only known to reproduce on PVWA version 10.10.10 (10.10.10.11)
known_issues.workaround: Ignore the improper message and enter a valid password per passparm.ini requirements found on the Vault server in <drive>:\Program Files (x86)\PrivateArk\Server\Conf

In the Windows event logs there will be a crash event in ntdll.dll with Exception code: 0xc0000374.

KI#00013287: Database sync following a DR replication can cause data loss
known_issues.description: The DB sync has a mechanism to help it run faster. A timestamp is saved every time the DR replicates from the Vault. When a DR failover occurs and the DB sync is run, all metadata for files newer than the saved timestamp are deleted. Common scenario that will trigger the data loss: 1. The Vault was running as an active Vault server. 2. The PrivateArk Server service on the Vault is stopped. 3. The CyberArk Vault Disaster Recovery service on the Vault is started. 4. A failover occurs before replication can be completed (normally due to replication error. 5. At this point, all metadata that is newer than the date the Vault last performed a successful replication will be removed. How to identify the issue: 1. The flow from the description above occurred. 2. In PADR.log on the affected Vault, you can see the following warning: "ITATS410W Deleting object *** from the Safe's directory. This object is a top version object, will attempt to replace it. "
known_issues.workaround: Perform Full replication from a known-good vault or Full restore from backup.

KI#00017388: PVWA Discovery task is stuck in pending state in case that the the CPM user that should run the task does not exist
known_issues.description: When creating a discovery task a Scanner (CPM) is attached to it (as the one that will execute the scan). In cases where the CPM user is deleted (does not exist anymore) the task will remain in pending state and no other task will be able to run. Steps to reproduce: 1. Create environment with 2 CPMs. 2. Delete the user of the first CPM (for example delete the user "PasswordManager" via the PrivateArk Client). 3. In the Discovery Management page in the PVWA, create a new onetime discovery task, use the first CPM user to run the task. 4. Create another discovery task, this time use the second CPM user to run the task. Current result: The discovery task of CPM1 will stuck in pending state and the other discovery task (of CPM2) will not be executed. Expected result: The discovery task of CPM1 will fail or or stay as pending state and the other discovery tasks of the other CPMs will be handled and executed.
known_issues.workaround: It is possible to delete the stuck tasks manually from the UI (PVWA->Discovery tasks)

known_issues.title: KI#00017704: PVWA crashed during cycled restart
known_issues.description: Issue Description PVWA crash, during it's automated restart. See example: The crash is correlated with the restart as in CyberArk.WebConsole.log When the CASOS tries to refresh credfile while the PVWA is shutting down, the PVWA crashes. It happens because the session used is no longer valid, so it recovers, but this fails the app since the ASM is already destroyed. Steps to Reproduce N/A Current Results PVWA crashed during service restart Expected Results PVWA restart should succeed without any issues. Error Messages N/A Logs w3wp.exe crash dumped is generated Other Symptoms N/A
known_issues.workaround: It's possible to minimize the chances of this race condition by changing the recycle period of PVWA to a value that isn't a multiple of 60 minutes. These are the steps: 1. open the IIS and go to application pools. 2. select the pvwa application pool - PasswordVaultWebAccessPool 3. open advanced settings 4. under Recycling, set the value of "Regular time interval" to 1770 (equals to 29.5 hours)

known_issues.title: KI#00014961: Dual Control: Inaccurate date sent in Request set manually (v10 UI)
KI#00017135: Vault shutdown is stalled if too many logon requests are queued up
known_issues.description: The vault has a queue for incoming requests. When shutdown starts, we continue performing all the requests that are already in the queue and then finalize the shutdown. In some cases, the requests in the queue are user logons. If many of these logon requests are from a single user in a short period of time (i.e. many PSM/PSMP connections opened at once), they are processed by the Vault in a serial manner. Once the requests are completed, the Vault services are able to stop. If there is a timeout during these logon requests, each logon request will take the time specified in the DefaultTimeout parameter in the Vault's DBParm.ini (by default, this is 30 seconds). Because multiple logons from the same user are processes serially, this can lead to long delays in the shutdown of the Vault (which also affects node failovers in HA Vault).
known_issues.workaround: Instruct users to avoid opening many simultaneous connections at once. If Vault is in middle of shutdown and is stuck working through requests, you may terminate the Vault process (only advised if shutdown of Vault is critical).

Steps to reproduce: # Create an account # in the new UI - open Accounts view # Go into the accounts details and notice the reconciliation date in the Compliance report # Open the account details in the old UI, and under "Advanced" tab notice the LastSuccessReconciliation property # Press the Move button from the old UI and move the account to another safe # Notice the LastSuccessReconciliation keep the same value # Go back to the Accounts view in the new UI and go into account details Expected: Compliance report on the account details for reconciliation/change time remains unchanged. known_issues.title: KI#00002594: On Exclusive Safes, Verify will always fail when the password is locked / checked out by another user
KI#00015113: PVWA Application's Bulk Upload CSV results in duplicate authIDs
known_issues.description: When using the Bulk Upload feature within the PVWA Applications page to add authentications into an existing Application, this will result in duplicate authID's. For this to happen there needs to an existing Application with already specified Authentications. Within the Bulk Upload CSV file, it needs to reference this same Application but with different Authentication types than what already exists. Doing this is does not consider the Authentiactions which already exist and adds authID's like there aren't any Authentications specified. This will result in duplicate authID's, this can be seen by querying the relevant Application using REST API, using the method "Get application authentication methods". For example: Existing Application with 2 OS user authentications: OSAuth1 - Id = 1 OSAuth2 - Id = 2 After uploading the Bulk Upload CSV with 2 Path Authentications it will now be: OSAuth1 - Id = 1 OsAuth2 - Id = 2 pathAuth - Id = 1 pathAuth - Id = 2 The main problem it causes is that when you want to delete an Authentication from an Application using the PVWA or the API you need to specify the Application ID and authID which can lead to multiple authentications being deleted.
known_issues.workaround: Don't use Bulk Upload CSV file for adding Authentications to an existing Application that has Authentications already.

KI#00016774: Vulnerability - Synchronizer Service Image path is missing quotes
known_issues.description: Issue Description: The synchronizer service image is unquoted and need to be fixed (during installation). for example: unquoted: C:\Program Files\CyberArk\Synchronizer\VaultConjurSynchronizer.exe should be fix to quoted: "C:\Program Files\CyberArk\Synchronizer\VaultConjurSynchronizer.exe" Component: Vault-Conjur Synchronizer Version: 10.10.3 and up Step to reproduce: 1. Install Vault Synchronizer 2. Run the following command in CMD: wmic service get name,displayname,pathname,startmode |findstr /i "auto" |findstr /i /v "c:\windows\\" |findstr /i /v """ 3. It will produce an output for services with unquoted imagePath, example: CyberArk Vault-Conjur Synchronizer CyberArkVaultConjurSynchronizer C:\Program Files\CyberArk\Synchronizer\VaultConjurSynchronizer.exe Auto 4. You can also verify the ImagePath in the registry: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\CyberArkVaultConjurSynchronizer Expected Results: The vault synchronizer image path should be quoted. Current Results: The ImagePath of the vault synchronizer is not quoted. If a vulnerability scanner is executed on the machine where it is installed on, it will pick it up and report the following: "The remote Windows host has at least one service installed that uses an unquoted service path"
known_issues.workaround: Manually fix it (after installation). Search for the unquoted registry entry of the affected service under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\CyberArkVaultConjurSynchronizer Double click the ImagePath key Add quotes " " at the start and end of the service path

KI#00014252: CRL access requirement
known_issues.description: In the CPM installation documentation on the page: Installation > Installing the PAS solution > Manual Installation > Enterprise Password Vault > Install CPM > Before Installation, url: [https://docs.cyberark.com/Product-Doc/OnlineHelp/PAS/Latest/en/Content/PAS%20INST/Before-Central-Policy-Installation.htm] The section "Enable a secure channel between CPM and PVWA" asks to Import the CA Certificate from the CA that issued the PVWA's SSL certificate to the CPM server. However, it does not mention that the CPM server also needs to be able to access the CRL (Certificate Revocation List) from the CRL Distribution Points in the certificate. If the CPM cannot access the CRL it may not accept the PVWA's certificate because it cannot verify that the certificate has not been revoked.
known_issues.workaround: To avoid this issue, make sure that the CPM server can access the CRL Distribution Points referenced in the PVWA's certificate.

known_issues.title: KI#00015454: HSM integration, Load Server Private Key causes Vault crash
known_issues.description: This is the scenario that was run when the bug was found, there may be other scenarios that will cause a bug too: HSM Vendor - nCipher nShield Connect (HSM as a Service) OS - Server 2016 The Vault will crash during start-up if the below is not set in the nCipher config file (cknfastrc): CKNFAST_OVERRIDE_SECURITY_ASSURANCES=longterm By default, any key which has been in nCipher's HSM for longer than 48 hours will be blocked as it will be considered insecure. Setting it to the above overrules this option. With the default setting the Vault will crash on start-up. nCipher pkcs11.log showing the error: === 2020-09-17 13:15:24 [4388] t4232: pkcs11-sam: 000008cc Application error: Key type CKK_AES label "Cyber-Ark Server Key" 2020-09-17 13:15:24 [4388] t4232: pkcs11-sam: 000008cc Application error: Insecure key being used too long after creation; set CKNFAST_OVERRIDE_SECURITY_ASSURANCES=longterm to allow 2020-09-17 13:15:24 [4388] t4232: pkcs11: 000008CC < rv 0x00000006 (CKR_FUNCTION_FAILED) === Whilst having the dbparm.ini parameter set to "DisableExceptionHandling=No" and CRYPT(1) Vault debugging enabled the following is seen within the traces: === 2020-09-17 12:54:15.049 00004132 pacrypto_pkcs11_v2_find_key found key and attached it. 2020-09-17 12:54:15.049 00004132 C_EncryptInit executing on SessionId=2252, Key Handle: 1118 2020-09-17 12:54:15.050 00004132 C_EncryptInit failed, SessionId=2252, Key Handle: 1118, Rv:6 2020-09-17 12:54:15.059 00004132 ITACM093E PKCS#11 device returned with error (6). … 2020-09-17 12:54:15.634 00004132 ITAGN001S System error (Code: 902). Exception code C0000005 at address 48101CA0 occurred in DB component === Dbmain.exe crash will be report both in the Windows Event System and Application logs.
known_issues.workaround: If using nCipher nShield Connect (HSM as a Service), edit the cknfastrc config file and either add or edit this file so you have the below present: CKNFAST_OVERRIDE_SECURITY_ASSURANCES=longterm This will allow the Vault to start successfully.

known_issues.title: KI#00015393: Get Account REST API - "nextLink" Link Is Broken
known_issues.description: When running the Get Accounts API method, if the 'Offset' parameter is used and the number of accounts exceeds the 'limit' parameter, at the end of the output a link is added that should generate the next query, to display the next results. This link does not function correctly. For example, if the user runs https://<PVWA>/PasswordVault/api/accounts?offset=0&limit=1000 The first 1000 accounts will be returned, and if there are more than 1000 accounts that fit the parameters in the call, at the end of the output there will be an additional line: "nextLink": "API/Accounts?offset=1000&limit=1000" This link is broken, however, as it's missing a necessary part of the URL (PasswordVault).
known_issues.workaround: Run the next query manually. For example if the original call was https://<PVWA>/PasswordVault/api/accounts?offset=0&limit=1000 Run https://<PVWA>/PasswordVault/api/accounts?offset=1000&limit=1000 to get the next 1000 accounts.

The Master was disabled because an unrecoverable error was detected. Code: 2105, Error: Error in reconcilepass to user TalDomain.com\Loooonnngggggggggggggggggggg on domain TalDomain.com. Reason: The user name could not be found. (winRc=2221). Reconcile account details - Safe: CPM, Folder: Root, Object: Conn Steps to reproduce: Add a domain user in Active Directory. known_issues.title: KI#00013840: Verify Change Reconcile immediate tasks appear stuck
known_issues.description: When submitting a Verify, Change, or Reconcile immediate task, the tasks do not get processed. This will happen for all accounts of like platform, while accounts of a different platform operate as expected. The CPM runs a Logic Container command each minute that selects the tasks which should be executed. It does this by querying only tasks which has a TimeToHandle < now (now is the unix timestamp of the Logic Container). This means that the command should never return a future value. In some situations we have found that the command returns a future value which results in this condition. To identify if you are being affected by this issue, the following PowerShell can be used. Simiply update the $path variable to point to the PMTrace log to analyse, then execute the script. If it returns output such as the following, this is an indication of the issue being experienced: logtime tid Incoming tasks time Start of operate line ------- --- ------------------- ---------------- ---- 10/04/2019 | 14:10:50.185591 3996 1554930605 1554923450 CACPM778I Immediate logic: Incoming tasks time: 1554930605, Last tasks time: 1554922979, Start of operate: 1554923450. logtime tid Platform ------- --- -------- 10/04/2019 | 07:23:33.180247 3996 <PlatformName> -----PowerShell Log Analysis Script below----- cls $path = "<path>\PMTrace.log" $pattern = "\[(.*?)\].*?{tid= +(\d+)}.*?CACPM778I.*?Incoming tasks time: (\d+),.*?Start of operate: (\d+)." $issues = Select-String -path $path -Pattern $pattern | ForEach-Object { If ($_.Matches.Groups[3].value -gt $_.Matches.Groups[4].value) { $props = [ordered]@{ 'logtime' = $_.Matches.Groups[1].value 'tid' = [int]($_.Matches.Groups[2].value) 'Incoming tasks time' = [int]($_.Matches.Groups[3].value) 'Start of operate' = [int]($_.Matches.Groups[4].value) 'line' = [regex]::Match($_.Line,'CACPM778I.*') } New-Object -TypeName PSObject -Property $props } } $issues | Sort-Object tid,'Start of operate' | Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap $issues.tid | Select-Object -Unique | Sort-Object | ForEach-Object { $pattern = "\[(.*?)\].*?{tid= +($_)}.*?CACPM657I Starting CPM policy cycle. Policy ID: (.*?)," Select-String -path $path -Pattern $pattern | ForEach-Object{ If (-NOT ($platform -eq $_.Matches.Groups[3].value)) { $platform = $_.Matches.Groups[3].value $props = [ordered]@{ 'logtime' = $_.Matches.Groups[1].value 'tid' = $_.Matches.Groups[2].value 'Platform' = $_.Matches.Groups[3].value } New-Object -TypeName PSObject -Property $props } } $platform = $null } | Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap
known_issues.workaround: Perform the following steps to reset the condition in which a future value is returned. 1) Restart the Logic Container service. 2) In cpm.ini, add this (manually, it is hidden in the PVWA ui) - RunSearchesOnStartup and restart he CPM service. Possible Values: None (Default - don't run searches, use the File Category values) Immediate (Run the immediate, ignore the File Category) Periodic (Same as Immediate, but for periodic) All (run everything - Immediate + Periodic)

known_issues.description: When opening IncomingRequests or MyRequests APIs, type for additionalInfo is incorrect, it's shown as object but it should be a dictionary. Steps to reproduce: Login to PVWA With Chrome DevTools > network tab open, navigate to Swagger UI Get the Swagger JSON from the v1 request and paste into https://editor.swagger.io/ May need to fix the URL value or Swagger version before it's rendered Open MyRequests or IncomingRequests and look for AdditionalInfo property Expected: Type for AdditionalInfo is correct (Dictionary) Actual: Structural error at paths./api/IncomingRequests/11.get.parameters.21.type should be equal to one of the allowed values allowedValues: string, number, boolean, integer, array
known_issues.workaround: No workaround

known_issues.title: KI#00015636: CPMStatus is 'failure' while verification is in progress
known_issues.description: When verifying an account, the CPM adds a File Category to the account called 'InProcess'. On PVWA side, the 'status' field in 'secretManagement' is determined by the 'CPMStatus' File Category of the account. This causes an visual issue in the PVWA and a programmatic issue when using the RestAPI and attempting to check the account status based on the 'status' field because when CPM begins management of a target account, one of the first actions is to set the CPMStatus to failure so that in the event of a catastrophic failure of the CPM, the account is correctly flagged as failed. The result; however, is that during the timeframe that CPM is actively managing the account, it is shown as failed in the PVWA UI and in RestAPI calls retrieving the account details.
known_issues.workaround: The "InProcess" file category can be seen using the WebServices RestAPI to get the account details. When a management activity is in process, it can be seen in the "InteralProperties" element of the call result. (https://docs.cyberark.com/Product-Doc/OnlineHelp/PAS/Latest/en/Content/WebServices/Get%20Account%20Details.htm) 1st Gen API (IE:) ... { "Key": "InProcess", "Value": "ChangeTask" }, ... ***Note: the "InProcess" file category will only exist on the account once CPM it in the process of managing it. Once management is complete it is removed.***

Reason: Data at the root level is invalid. Line 1, position 1| |2|an unexpected error occurred|'<some char>' is an unexpected token. the expected token is '<some char>' Line X position X| |3|the application details will open but the 2 checkbox under the authentications tab will be checked and block from editing. also all authentications will be blocked except os user and allowed machines that will return error of not allowed when trying to add these authemtications.|no error except adding authentications: you are not allowed to add this authentication to application| *Steps to reproduce:* Create two scripts that add several authentications for the same app and run both in the same time. In Vault, the mysql command "_select * from vault_users_metadata_" shows trimmed XML records.

This will happen when all the following points are true for an account: 1. The account is not managed by CPM 2. On the account's platform level there is a linked account that has a Display Name defined 3. The linked account is first on the 'Linked Accounts' list 4. The linked account is not a logon account
known_issues.workaround: In the PVWA click on Administration > Platform Management and edit the relevant platform. Click on UI & Workflows > Linked accounts. If one of the linked accounts is a Logon Account - Set the Logon Account to be the first one in this list by right clicking on LogonAccount and entry and clicking on 'Move Up' until it is the first one). If no Logon Account is set on the platform - Remove the "Display name" value from the other linked accounts in the platform.

Setup a Platform with Dual Control and Multi-Level Password Access. 2. Add the following users to the safe: Approval 1 - L1 Approval with List Permission Approval 2 - L2 Approval with List Permission User 1 - Use Permission User 2 - List Permission 3. But, you will see the CC is to Approval 1 & Approval 2 who has List Permission. Current Results Only Approval User receives the e-mail notification. Expected Results All user with permission configured in the ENE Recipient should receive the e-mail notification. Error Messages N/A Logs N/A Other Symptoms N/A

known_issues.title: KI#00011897: Misleading trace record after successful approval of request
known_issues.description: Issue Description Misleading trace record (ITATS312E) after successful approval of request Steps to Reproduce 1. Configure Dual Control for a Platform. 2. Configure the following permissions for a Safe: * ReqUser as a requester user with basic permissions on the safe * ApproveUser as the approver limited to these permissions only on the respected safe: - List accounts. - Authorize account request. 3. Create an account in the safe. 4. Login with ReqUser and request access to the account. 5. Login with ApproveUser and approve the request. 6. You will see ITATS312E is logged in Vault trace. Current Results ITATS312E is logged in Vault trace. Expected Results Vault trace logs should not show any error. Error Messages N/A Logs The following is logged in Vault trace (trace.d*): ITATS312E ApproveUser: You are unauthorized to manage Users. Other Symptoms N/A

known_issues.title: KI#00015374: AddressSeparatorCharacter needs to be used to separate addresses in Connect Request.
known_issues.description: Issue Description AddressSeparatorCharacter needs to be used to separate addresses in Connect Request. Some platforms may require addresses to be in format of [IP],[port]. For example, MS SQL Server where PSM-SSMS needs the PSMRemoteMachine in the format of [ip],[Port]. In order for PVWA to be able to process a request correctly and separate the servers that a user is requesting access to - we have a property named AddressSeparatorCharacter. The issue is when a user needs to select from a list of pre-defined addresses. The PVWA (v9/v10 UI) always creates the request using commas between addresses, regardless of AddressSeparatorCharacter value. If AddressSeparatorCharacter is set to semicolon and 2 addresses are selected the UI will separate them with a comma and as a result after the request is confirmed the user will not have a choice of addresses, but it will try to connect to a concatenated address. Steps to Reproduce 1. Setup an environment with PSM and Dual Control. 2. Create an account in "WinDomain" platform. Under "Remote machine access", put in 2 values (need to write them in seperate lines) 3. In PVWA Options > Privileged Account Request > change "AddressSeparatorCharacter" to a semi-colon ";". 4. Login to PVWA a weak account and request to connect to this account. In the request select both addresses (notice the UI separates them with comma and not a semi-colon). 5. Login to PVWA with a permitted user and approve the request. 6. Login to PVWA with the weak account and click "Connect". Current Results PVWA (v9/v10 UI) separates the Address and Port, resulting in 2 separate entries. Expected Results PVWA (v9/v10 UI) should treat Address,Port as a single Address (similar as when the request is created) Error Messages N/A Logs N/A Other Symptoms N/A
known_issues.workaround: If the user is forced to change the AddressSeparatorCharacter (for instance - a DB requires to get address parameters in form of [IP],[port]) for requests that he/she can workaround this issue by creating a separate account for each address they wish to add to a restricted address list. For PVWA v9 UI, there is no workaround. User should use v10 to initiate the Request Connection / Request Show / Request Copy.

KI#00015032: PSM Cannot create more than one RemoteApp connection from the same MAC client
known_issues.description: When using the RDP client on a MAC and attempting to connect more than one RemoteApp session, it fails and all open sessions on that workstation are closed. When only one session is created there is no issue. The issue is only for RemoteApp from MAC devices and only when the sessions are from the same client machine. Steps to reproduce: 1. Create a PVWA, PSM environment with version 11.4 2. Turn on RemoteApp 3. From a Mac machine, download Windows Remote Desktop from the app store 4. From the Mac terminal console run the following commands: defaults write com.microsoft.rdc.macos ClientSettings.EnforceCredSSPSupport 0 5. Login to the PVWA, find a Unix platform account (or any account on a platform with RemoteApp not disabled by default) and download the RDP file. 6. Open it with Windows Remote Desktop 7. It connects without any issue 8. Download an other RemoteApp-enabled RDP connection file 9. Open it with Windows Remote Desktop 10. The error "Timeout has expired, User is being disconnected" is displayed and it closes the first connection as well.
known_issues.workaround: The current work around is to disable the RemoteApp feature. This can be done in one of three ways: 1. Globally: In the PVWA, navigate to Configuration Options > Options > Privileged Session Management UI, and set UseRemoteApp to No 2. By connector globally: In the PVWA, navigate to Configuration Options > Options > Connection Components. Expand the desired connector, right-click on Component Parameters, and select "Add Parameter". Add a parameter called DisableRemoteApp and set its value to "Yes". 3. By connector per platform: In the PVWA, navigate to Configuration Options > Platform Management. Select and edit the desired platform, expand to UI & Workflfow > Connection Components, and expand the desired connection component. Add a parameter to Override Component Parameters called DisableRemoteApp and set its value to "Yes".

known_issues.title: KI#00015056: Custom account properties can not be removed via REST API
known_issues.description: Custom account properties that are defined as "Internal" properties in the pvwa configuration can be added and updated via accounts REST API but can't be removed. When attempting to remove using the RestAPI Accounts Patch method. The call completes successfully, but the property is not removed. Steps to reproduce: * Add custom properties for a policy * Add account with a custom property defined * Ensure the custom property is listed in the internal properties configuration of the PVWA * Attempt to remove the property via Accounts PATCH REST API Expected result: PATCH is successful and property is removed Actual result: PATCH is successful but property isn't removed
known_issues.workaround: Remove property from the list of internal properties in PVWA Configuration Patch Account again and remove property

known_issues.title: KI#00016344: Syslog does not support the ';' character in the Command (or any other ExtraDetails field) value
KI#00001484: When the target account is missing its username but has a logon account, the username of the logon account is used for the tasks verify, change and reconcile.
KI#00016309: TPC: Parameter 'pmextrapass3' is mandatory but does not exist or has an empty value even though Reocncile account assigned and has password
known_issues.description: When using TPC on a platform that does not utilize the "username" file category (such as the SunOne Directory platform), Reconcile actions may fail with error "ERROR -> bc :: f -> Parameter 'pmextrapass3' is mandatory but does not exist or has an empty value" even though the target account has a reconcile account associated and that reconcile account does have a valid password. This happens because CPM does not load the reconcile account data into the plugin's temp file when there is no "username" value found for the Reconcile account.
known_issues.workaround: 1) Add "username" to the platform configuration for the Reconcile Account as an optional value a. Edit the platform of the reconcile account b. Browse to (UI & Workflows -> Properties -> Optional) c. Right-click "Optional" and select Add Property d. Set Name value to Username e. Apply the changes 2) Then give the Reconcile Account a username value. The value can be anything like "placeholder". It just needs to have a value for the reconcile account information to be passed to the plugin.

known_issues.title: KI#00014137: When Satellite connectivity to the Master Vault is restored, may cause the Satellite Vault to crash
KI#00015089: Performance issue with "Developer Application" mechanism on Windows
KI#00012538: Vault transactions fail with error code 'ITAPE266E'
known_issues.description: Some communication related transaction may fail with the below error code: ITAPE266E Send object failed (code = 3, 0) Currently error ITAPE266E isn't defined as a communication error therefore there is no attempt for zero sockets recovery is made when it is encountered. This will then cause the relevant transaction to fail, due to no recovery process. Depending on the type of transaction this will have low to no product impact. Bug will often be seen in "GetFile" transactions against configuration files. This can have high impact against a long running "GenerateFullBackup" transaction. This is the transaction used when a DR Vault initiates a full replication. In the PADR.log on the DR Vault, you would see the following errors: === CASTM003E Vault transaction failed. Reason: ITAPE266E Send object failed (code = 3, 0) . ITAPE266E Send object failed (code = 3, 0) PADR0005E Full Replicate failed. (Code -1409266) PADR0005E Reset Slave failed (Code -1409266) PADR0011E Replicate failed. === For the impact against the "GenerateFullBackup" transaction, this can often be seen in cases where a firewall TCP timeout has been configured which will end the network tunnel between the Primary Vault and the DR Vault. Issue would happen where the full replication takes longer than the specified firewall TCP timeout. For example, firewall has been configured to have a TCP timeout of 1 hour, but the full replication takes 5 hours. The tunnel would be disconnected between the two Vault's servers causing the error code 'ITAPE266E'.
known_issues.workaround: We do not have a workaround available yet. If this is affecting 'GenerateFullBackup' transactions, you can increase the firewall TCP timeout value to ensure it covers the full replication time so it doesn't break the communication tunnel.
